*\n *\t\tQUERY:\n *\t\t\t\tINSERT STATEMENTS\n *\n *****************************************************************************/\n\nInsertStmt:\n\t\t\topt_with_clause INSERT INTO insert_target insert_rest\n\t\t\topt_on_conflict returning_clause\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$5->relation = $4;\n\t\t\t\t\t$5->onConflictClause = $6;\n\t\t\t\t\t$5->returningClause = $7;\n\t\t\t\t\t$5->withClause = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) $5;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\n/*\n * Can't easily make AS optional here, because VALUES in insert_rest would\n * have a shift/reduce conflict with VALUES as an optional alias.  We could\n * easily allow unreserved_keywords as optional aliases, but that'd be an odd\n * divergence from other places.  So just require AS for now.\n */\ninsert_target:\n\t\t\tqualified_name\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = $1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| qualified_name AS ColId\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$1->alias = makeAlias($3, NIL);\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = $1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\ninsert_rest:\n\t\t\tSelectStmt\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeNode(InsertStmt);\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->cols = NIL;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->selectStmt = $1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| OVERRIDING override_kind VALUE_P SelectStmt\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeNode(InsertStmt);\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->cols = NIL;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->override = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->selectStmt = $4;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| '(' insert_column_list ')' SelectStmt\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeNode(InsertStmt);\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->cols = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->selectStmt = $4;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| '(' insert_column_list ')' OVERRIDING override_kind VALUE_P SelectStmt\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeNode(InsertStmt);\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->cols = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->override = $5;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->selectStmt = $7;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| DEFAULT VALUES\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeNode(InsertStmt);\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->cols = NIL;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->selectStmt = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\noverride_kind:\n\t\t\tUSER\t\t{ $$ = OVERRIDING_USER_VALUE; }\n\t\t\t| SYSTEM_P\t{ $$ = OVERRIDING_SYSTEM_VALUE; }\n\t\t;\n\ninsert_column_list:\n\t\t\tinsert_column_item\n\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = list_make1($1); }\n\t\t\t| insert_column_list ',' insert_column_item\n\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = lappend($1, $3); }\n\t\t;\n\ninsert_column_item:\n\t\t\tColId opt_indirection\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeNode(ResTarget);\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->name = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->indirection = check_indirection($2, yyscanner);\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->val = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\nopt_on_conflict:\n\t\t\tON CONFLICT opt_conf_expr DO UPDATE SET set_clause_list\twhere_clause\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeNode(OnConflictClause);\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->action = ONCONFLICT_UPDATE;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->infer = $3;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->targetList = $7;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->whereClause = $8;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t|\n\t\t\tON CONFLICT opt_conf_expr DO NOTHING\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeNode(OnConflictClause);\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->action = ONCONFLICT_NOTHING;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->infer = $3;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->targetList = NIL;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->whereClause = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| /*EMPTY*/\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\nopt_conf_expr:\n\t\t\t'(' index_params ')' where_clause\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeNode(InferClause);\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->indexElems = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->whereClause = $4;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->conname = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t|\n\t\t\tON CONSTRAINT name\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeNode(InferClause);\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->indexElems = NIL;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->whereClause = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->conname = $3;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| /*EMPTY*/\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\nreturning_clause:\n\t\t\tRETURNING returning_with_clause target_list\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tReturningClause *n = makeNode(ReturningClause);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->options = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->exprs = $3;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| /* EMPTY */\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\nreturning_with_clause:\n\t\t\tWITH '(' returning_options ')'\t\t{ $$ = $3; }\n\t\t\t| /* EMPTY */\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = NIL; }\n\t\t;\n\nreturning_options:\n\t\t\treturning_option\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = list_make1($1); }\n\t\t\t| returning_options ',' returning_option\t{ $$ = lappend($1, $3); }\n\t\t;\n\nreturning_option:\n\t\t\treturning_option_kind AS ColId\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tReturningOption *n = makeNode(ReturningOption);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->option = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->value = $3;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\nreturning_option_kind:\n\t\t\tOLD\t\t\t{ $$ = RETURNING_OPTION_OLD; }\n\t\t\t| NEW\t\t{ $$ = RETURNING_OPTION_NEW; }\n\t\t;\n\n\n/*****************************************************************************\n *\n *\t\tQUERY:\n *\t\t\t\tDELETE STATEMENTS\n *\n *****************************************************************************/\n\nDeleteStmt: opt_with_clause DELETE_P FROM relation_expr_opt_alias\n\t\t\tusing_clause where_or_current_clause returning_clause\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tDeleteStmt *n = makeNode(DeleteStmt);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->relation = $4;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->usingClause = $5;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->whereClause = $6;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->returningClause = $7;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->withClause = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\nusing_clause:\n\t\t\t\tUSING from_list\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $2; }\n\t\t\t| /*EMPTY*/\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = NIL; }\n\t\t;\n\n\n/*****************************************************************************\n *\n *\t\tQUERY:\n *\t\t\t\tLOCK TABLE\n *\n *****************************************************************************/\n\nLockStmt:\tLOCK_P opt_table relation_expr_list opt_lock opt_nowait\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tLockStmt   *n = makeNode(LockStmt);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->relations = $3;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->mode = $4;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->nowait = $5;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\nopt_lock:\tIN_P lock_type MODE\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $2; }\n\t\t\t| /*EMPTY*/\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = AccessExclusiveLock; }\n\t\t;\n\nlock_type:\tACCESS SHARE\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = AccessShareLock; }\n\t\t\t| ROW SHARE\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = RowShareLock; }\n\t\t\t| ROW EXCLUSIVE\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = RowExclusiveLock; }\n\t\t\t| SHARE UPDATE EXCLUSIVE\t\t{ $$ = ShareUpdateExclusiveLock; }\n\t\t\t| SHARE\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = ShareLock; }\n\t\t\t| SHARE ROW EXCLUSIVE\t\t\t{ $$ = ShareRowExclusiveLock; }\n\t\t\t| EXCLUSIVE\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = ExclusiveLock; }\n\t\t\t| ACCESS EXCLUSIVE\t\t\t\t{ $$ = AccessExclusiveLock; }\n\t\t;\n\nopt_nowait:\tNOWAIT\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = true; }\n\t\t\t| /*EMPTY*/\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = false; }\n\t\t;\n\nopt_nowait_or_skip:\n\t\t\tNOWAIT\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = LockWaitError; }\n\t\t\t| SKIP LOCKED\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = LockWaitSkip; }\n\t\t\t| /*EMPTY*/\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = LockWaitBlock; }\n\t\t;\n\n\n/*****************************************************************************\n *\n *\t\tQUERY:\n *\t\t\t\tUpdateStmt (UPDATE)\n *\n *****************************************************************************/\n\nUpdateStmt: opt_with_clause UPDATE relation_expr_opt_alias\n\t\t\tSET set_clause_list\n\t\t\tfrom_clause\n\t\t\twhere_or_current_clause\n\t\t\treturning_clause\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tUpdateStmt *n = makeNode(UpdateStmt);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->relation = $3;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->targetList = $5;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->fromClause = $6;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->whereClause = $7;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->returningClause = $8;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->withClause = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\nset_clause_list:\n\t\t\tset_clause\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| set_clause_list ',' set_clause\t{ $$ = list_concat($1,$3); }\n\t\t;\n\nset_clause:\n\t\t\tset_target '=' a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$1->val = (Node *) $3;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = list_make1($1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| '(' set_target_list ')' '=' a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tint\t\t\tncolumns = list_length($2);\n\t\t\t\t\tint\t\t\ti = 1;\n\t\t\t\t\tListCell   *col_cell;\n\n\t\t\t\t\t/* Create a MultiAssignRef source for each target */\n\t\t\t\t\tforeach(col_cell, $2)\n\t\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t\tResTarget  *res_col = (ResTarget *) lfirst(col_cell);\n\t\t\t\t\t\tMultiAssignRef *r = makeNode(MultiAssignRef);\n\n\t\t\t\t\t\tr->source = (Node *) $5;\n\t\t\t\t\t\tr->colno = i;\n\t\t\t\t\t\tr->ncolumns = ncolumns;\n\t\t\t\t\t\tres_col->val = (Node *) r;\n\t\t\t\t\t\ti++;\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = $2;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\nset_target:\n\t\t\tColId opt_indirection\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeNode(ResTarget);\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->name = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->indirection = check_indirection($2, yyscanner);\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->val = NULL;\t/* upper production sets this */\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\nset_target_list:\n\t\t\tset_target\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = list_make1($1); }\n\t\t\t| set_target_list ',' set_target\t\t{ $$ = lappend($1,$3); }\n\t\t;\n\n\n/*****************************************************************************\n *\n *\t\tQUERY:\n *\t\t\t\tMERGE\n *\n *****************************************************************************/\n\nMergeStmt:\n\t\t\topt_with_clause MERGE INTO relation_expr_opt_alias\n\t\t\tUSING table_ref\n\t\t\tON a_expr\n\t\t\tmerge_when_list\n\t\t\treturning_clause\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tMergeStmt  *m = makeNode(MergeStmt);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tm->withClause = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\tm->relation = $4;\n\t\t\t\t\tm->sourceRelation = $6;\n\t\t\t\t\tm->joinCondition = $8;\n\t\t\t\t\tm->mergeWhenClauses = $9;\n\t\t\t\t\tm->returningClause = $10;\n\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) m;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\nmerge_when_list:\n\t\t\tmerge_when_clause\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = list_make1($1); }\n\t\t\t| merge_when_list merge_when_clause\t\t{ $$ = lappend($1,$2); }\n\t\t;\n\n/*\n * A WHEN clause may be WHEN MATCHED, WHEN NOT MATCHED BY SOURCE, or WHEN NOT\n * MATCHED [BY TARGET]. The first two cases match target tuples, and support\n * UPDATE/DELETE/DO NOTHING actions. The third case does not match target\n * tuples, and only supports INSERT/DO NOTHING actions.\n */\nmerge_when_clause:\n\t\t\tmerge_when_tgt_matched opt_merge_when_condition THEN merge_update\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$4->matchKind = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$4->condition = $2;\n\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) $4;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| merge_when_tgt_matched opt_merge_when_condition THEN merge_delete\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$4->matchKind = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$4->condition = $2;\n\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) $4;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| merge_when_tgt_not_matched opt_merge_when_condition THEN merge_insert\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$4->matchKind = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$4->condition = $2;\n\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) $4;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| merge_when_tgt_matched opt_merge_when_condition THEN DO NOTHING\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tMergeWhenClause *m = makeNode(MergeWhenClause);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tm->matchKind = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\tm->commandType = CMD_NOTHING;\n\t\t\t\t\tm->condition = $2;\n\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) m;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| merge_when_tgt_not_matched opt_merge_when_condition THEN DO NOTHING\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tMergeWhenClause *m = makeNode(MergeWhenClause);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tm->matchKind = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\tm->commandType = CMD_NOTHING;\n\t\t\t\t\tm->condition = $2;\n\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) m;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\nmerge_when_tgt_matched:\n\t\t\tWHEN MATCHED\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = MERGE_WHEN_MATCHED; }\n\t\t\t| WHEN NOT MATCHED BY SOURCE\t{ $$ = MERGE_WHEN_NOT_MATCHED_BY_SOURCE; }\n\t\t;\n\nmerge_when_tgt_not_matched:\n\t\t\tWHEN NOT MATCHED\t\t\t\t{ $$ = MERGE_WHEN_NOT_MATCHED_BY_TARGET; }\n\t\t\t| WHEN NOT MATCHED BY TARGET\t{ $$ = MERGE_WHEN_NOT_MATCHED_BY_TARGET; }\n\t\t;\n\nopt_merge_when_condition:\n\t\t\tAND a_expr\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $2; }\n\t\t\t|\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = NULL; }\n\t\t;\n\nmerge_update:\n\t\t\tUPDATE SET set_clause_list\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tMergeWhenClause *n = makeNode(MergeWhenClause);\n\t\t\t\t\tn->commandType = CMD_UPDATE;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->override = OVERRIDING_NOT_SET;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->targetList = $3;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->values = NIL;\n\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\nmerge_delete:\n\t\t\tDELETE_P\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tMergeWhenClause *n = makeNode(MergeWhenClause);\n\t\t\t\t\tn->commandType = CMD_DELETE;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->override = OVERRIDING_NOT_SET;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->targetList = NIL;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->values = NIL;\n\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\nmerge_insert:\n\t\t\tINSERT merge_values_clause\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tMergeWhenClause *n = makeNode(MergeWhenClause);\n\t\t\t\t\tn->commandType = CMD_INSERT;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->override = OVERRIDING_NOT_SET;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->targetList = NIL;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->values = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| INSERT OVERRIDING override_kind VALUE_P merge_values_clause\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tMergeWhenClause *n = makeNode(MergeWhenClause);\n\t\t\t\t\tn->commandType = CMD_INSERT;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->override = $3;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->targetList = NIL;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->values = $5;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| INSERT '(' insert_column_list ')' merge_values_clause\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tMergeWhenClause *n = makeNode(MergeWhenClause);\n\t\t\t\t\tn->commandType = CMD_INSERT;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->override = OVERRIDING_NOT_SET;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->targetList = $3;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->values = $5;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| INSERT '(' insert_column_list ')' OVERRIDING override_kind VALUE_P merge_values_clause\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tMergeWhenClause *n = makeNode(MergeWhenClause);\n\t\t\t\t\tn->commandType = CMD_INSERT;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->override = $6;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->targetList = $3;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->values = $8;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| INSERT DEFAULT VALUES\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tMergeWhenClause *n = makeNode(MergeWhenClause);\n\t\t\t\t\tn->commandType = CMD_INSERT;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->override = OVERRIDING_NOT_SET;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->targetList = NIL;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->values = NIL;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\nmerge_values_clause:\n\t\t\tVALUES '(' expr_list ')'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = $3;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\n/*****************************************************************************\n *\n *\t\tQUERY:\n *\t\t\t\tCURSOR STATEMENTS\n *\n *****************************************************************************/\nDeclareCursorStmt: DECLARE cursor_name cursor_options CURSOR opt_hold FOR SelectStmt\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tDeclareCursorStmt *n = makeNode(DeclareCursorStmt);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->portalname = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\t/* currently we always set FAST_PLAN option */\n\t\t\t\t\tn->options = $3 | $5 | CURSOR_OPT_FAST_PLAN;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->query = $7;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\ncursor_name:\tname\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t;\n\ncursor_options: /*EMPTY*/\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = 0; }\n\t\t\t| cursor_options NO SCROLL\t\t{ $$ = $1 | CURSOR_OPT_NO_SCROLL; }\n\t\t\t| cursor_options SCROLL\t\t\t{ $$ = $1 | CURSOR_OPT_SCROLL; }\n\t\t\t| cursor_options BINARY\t\t\t{ $$ = $1 | CURSOR_OPT_BINARY; }\n\t\t\t| cursor_options ASENSITIVE\t\t{ $$ = $1 | CURSOR_OPT_ASENSITIVE; }\n\t\t\t| cursor_options INSENSITIVE\t{ $$ = $1 | CURSOR_OPT_INSENSITIVE; }\n\t\t;\n\nopt_hold: /* EMPTY */\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = 0; }\n\t\t\t| WITH HOLD\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = CURSOR_OPT_HOLD; }\n\t\t\t| WITHOUT HOLD\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = 0; }\n\t\t;\n\n/*****************************************************************************\n *\n *\t\tQUERY:\n *\t\t\t\tSELECT STATEMENTS\n *\n *****************************************************************************/\n\n/* A complete SELECT statement looks like this.\n *\n * The rule returns either a single SelectStmt node or a tree of them,\n * representing a set-operation tree.\n *\n * There is an ambiguity when a sub-SELECT is within an a_expr and there\n * are excess parentheses: do the parentheses belong to the sub-SELECT or\n * to the surrounding a_expr?  We don't really care, but bison wants to know.\n * To resolve the ambiguity, we are careful to define the grammar so that\n * the decision is staved off as long as possible: as long as we can keep\n * absorbing parentheses into the sub-SELECT, we will do so, and only when\n * it's no longer possible to do that will we decide that parens belong to\n * the expression.\tFor example, in \"SELECT (((SELECT 2)) + 3)\" the extra\n * parentheses are treated as part of the sub-select.  The necessity of doing\n * it that way is shown by \"SELECT (((SELECT 2)) UNION SELECT 2)\".\tHad we\n * parsed \"((SELECT 2))\" as an a_expr, it'd be too late to go back to the\n * SELECT viewpoint when we see the UNION.\n *\n * This approach is implemented by defining a nonterminal select_with_parens,\n * which represents a SELECT with at least one outer layer of parentheses,\n * and being careful to use select_with_parens, never '(' SelectStmt ')',\n * in the expression grammar.  We will then have shift-reduce conflicts\n * which we can resolve in favor of always treating '(' <select> ')' as\n * a select_with_parens.  To resolve the conflicts, the productions that\n * conflict with the select_with_parens productions are manually given\n * precedences lower than the precedence of ')', thereby ensuring that we\n * shift ')' (and then reduce to select_with_parens) rather than trying to\n * reduce the inner <select> nonterminal to something else.  We use UMINUS\n * precedence for this, which is a fairly arbitrary choice.\n *\n * To be able to define select_with_parens itself without ambiguity, we need\n * a nonterminal select_no_parens that represents a SELECT structure with no\n * outermost parentheses.  This is a little bit tedious, but it works.\n *\n * In non-expression contexts, we use SelectStmt which can represent a SELECT\n * with or without outer parentheses.\n */\n\nSelectStmt: select_no_parens\t\t\t%prec UMINUS\n\t\t\t| select_with_parens\t\t%prec UMINUS\n\t\t;\n\nselect_with_parens:\n\t\t\t'(' select_no_parens ')'\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $2; }\n\t\t\t| '(' select_with_parens ')'\t\t\t{ $$ = $2; }\n\t\t;\n\n/*\n * This rule parses the equivalent of the standard's <query expression>.\n * The duplicative productions are annoying, but hard to get rid of without\n * creating shift/reduce conflicts.\n *\n *\tThe locking clause (FOR UPDATE etc) may be before or after LIMIT/OFFSET.\n *\tIn <=7.2.X, LIMIT/OFFSET had to be after FOR UPDATE\n *\tWe now support both orderings, but prefer LIMIT/OFFSET before the locking\n * clause.\n *\t2002-08-28 bjm\n */\nselect_no_parens:\n\t\t\tsimple_select\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| select_clause sort_clause\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tinsertSelectOptions((SelectStmt *) $1, $2, NIL,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tNULL, NULL,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tyyscanner);\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = $1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| select_clause opt_sort_clause for_locking_clause opt_select_limit\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tinsertSelectOptions((SelectStmt *) $1, $2, $3,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t$4,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tNULL,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tyyscanner);\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = $1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| select_clause opt_sort_clause select_limit opt_for_locking_clause\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tinsertSelectOptions((SelectStmt *) $1, $2, $4,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t$3,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tNULL,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tyyscanner);\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = $1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| with_clause select_clause\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tinsertSelectOptions((SelectStmt *) $2, NULL, NIL,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tNULL,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t$1,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tyyscanner);\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = $2;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| with_clause select_clause sort_clause\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tinsertSelectOptions((SelectStmt *) $2, $3, NIL,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tNULL,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t$1,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tyyscanner);\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = $2;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| with_clause select_clause opt_sort_clause for_locking_clause opt_select_limit\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tinsertSelectOptions((SelectStmt *) $2, $3, $4,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t$5,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t$1,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tyyscanner);\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = $2;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| with_clause select_clause opt_sort_clause select_limit opt_for_locking_clause\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tinsertSelectOptions((SelectStmt *) $2, $3, $5,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t$4,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t$1,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tyyscanner);\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = $2;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\nselect_clause:\n\t\t\tsimple_select\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| select_with_parens\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t;\n\n/*\n * This rule parses SELECT statements that can appear within set operations,\n * including UNION, INTERSECT and EXCEPT.  '(' and ')' can be used to specify\n * the ordering of the set operations.\tWithout '(' and ')' we want the\n * operations to be ordered per the precedence specs at the head of this file.\n *\n * As with select_no_parens, simple_select cannot have outer parentheses,\n * but can have parenthesized subclauses.\n *\n * It might appear that we could fold the first two alternatives into one\n * by using opt_distinct_clause.  However, that causes a shift/reduce conflict\n * against INSERT ... SELECT ... ON CONFLICT.  We avoid the ambiguity by\n * requiring SELECT DISTINCT [ON] to be followed by a non-empty target_list.\n *\n * Note that sort clauses cannot be included at this level --- SQL requires\n *\t\tSELECT foo UNION SELECT bar ORDER BY baz\n * to be parsed as\n *\t\t(SELECT foo UNION SELECT bar) ORDER BY baz\n * not\n *\t\tSELECT foo UNION (SELECT bar ORDER BY baz)\n * Likewise for WITH, FOR UPDATE and LIMIT.  Therefore, those clauses are\n * described as part of the select_no_parens production, not simple_select.\n * This does not limit functionality, because you can reintroduce these\n * clauses inside parentheses.\n *\n * NOTE: only the leftmost component SelectStmt should have INTO.\n * However, this is not checked by the grammar; parse analysis must check it.\n */\nsimple_select:\n\t\t\tSELECT opt_all_clause opt_target_list\n\t\t\tinto_clause from_clause where_clause\n\t\t\tgroup_clause having_clause window_clause\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tSelectStmt *n = makeNode(SelectStmt);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->targetList = $3;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->intoClause = $4;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->fromClause = $5;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->whereClause = $6;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->groupClause = ($7)->list;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->groupDistinct = ($7)->distinct;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->havingClause = $8;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->windowClause = $9;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| SELECT distinct_clause target_list\n\t\t\tinto_clause from_clause where_clause\n\t\t\tgroup_clause having_clause window_clause\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tSelectStmt *n = makeNode(SelectStmt);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->distinctClause = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->targetList = $3;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->intoClause = $4;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->fromClause = $5;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->whereClause = $6;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->groupClause = ($7)->list;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->groupDistinct = ($7)->distinct;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->havingClause = $8;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->windowClause = $9;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| values_clause\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| TABLE relation_expr\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t/* same as SELECT * FROM relation_expr */\n\t\t\t\t\tColumnRef  *cr = makeNode(ColumnRef);\n\t\t\t\t\tResTarget  *rt = makeNode(ResTarget);\n\t\t\t\t\tSelectStmt *n = makeNode(SelectStmt);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tcr->fields = list_make1(makeNode(A_Star));\n\t\t\t\t\tcr->location = -1;\n\n\t\t\t\t\trt->name = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\trt->indirection = NIL;\n\t\t\t\t\trt->val = (Node *) cr;\n\t\t\t\t\trt->location = -1;\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->targetList = list_make1(rt);\n\t\t\t\t\tn->fromClause = list_make1($2);\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| select_clause UNION set_quantifier select_clause\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeSetOp(SETOP_UNION, $3 == SET_QUANTIFIER_ALL, $1, $4);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| select_clause INTERSECT set_quantifier select_clause\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeSetOp(SETOP_INTERSECT, $3 == SET_QUANTIFIER_ALL, $1, $4);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| select_clause EXCEPT set_quantifier select_clause\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeSetOp(SETOP_EXCEPT, $3 == SET_QUANTIFIER_ALL, $1, $4);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\n/*\n * SQL standard WITH clause looks like:\n *\n * WITH [ RECURSIVE ] <query name> [ (<column>,...) ]\n *\t\tAS (query) [ SEARCH or CYCLE clause ]\n *\n * Recognizing WITH_LA here allows a CTE to be named TIME or ORDINALITY.\n */\nwith_clause:\n\t\tWITH cte_list\n\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t$$ = makeNode(WithClause);\n\t\t\t\t$$->ctes = $2;\n\t\t\t\t$$->recursive = false;\n\t\t\t\t$$->location = @1;\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t| WITH_LA cte_list\n\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t$$ = makeNode(WithClause);\n\t\t\t\t$$->ctes = $2;\n\t\t\t\t$$->recursive = false;\n\t\t\t\t$$->location = @1;\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t| WITH RECURSIVE cte_list\n\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t$$ = makeNode(WithClause);\n\t\t\t\t$$->ctes = $3;\n\t\t\t\t$$->recursive = true;\n\t\t\t\t$$->location = @1;\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\ncte_list:\n\t\tcommon_table_expr\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = list_make1($1); }\n\t\t| cte_list ',' common_table_expr\t\t{ $$ = lappend($1, $3); }\n\t\t;\n\ncommon_table_expr:  name opt_name_list AS opt_materialized '(' PreparableStmt ')' opt_search_clause opt_cycle_clause\n\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\tCommonTableExpr *n = makeNode(CommonTableExpr);\n\n\t\t\t\tn->ctename = $1;\n\t\t\t\tn->aliascolnames = $2;\n\t\t\t\tn->ctematerialized = $4;\n\t\t\t\tn->ctequery = $6;\n\t\t\t\tn->search_clause = castNode(CTESearchClause, $8);\n\t\t\t\tn->cycle_clause = castNode(CTECycleClause, $9);\n\t\t\t\tn->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\nopt_materialized:\n\t\tMATERIALIZED\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = CTEMaterializeAlways; }\n\t\t| NOT MATERIALIZED\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = CTEMaterializeNever; }\n\t\t| /*EMPTY*/\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = CTEMaterializeDefault; }\n\t\t;\n\nopt_search_clause:\n\t\tSEARCH DEPTH FIRST_P BY columnList SET ColId\n\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\tCTESearchClause *n = makeNode(CTESearchClause);\n\n\t\t\t\tn->search_col_list = $5;\n\t\t\t\tn->search_breadth_first = false;\n\t\t\t\tn->search_seq_column = $7;\n\t\t\t\tn->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t| SEARCH BREADTH FIRST_P BY columnList SET ColId\n\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\tCTESearchClause *n = makeNode(CTESearchClause);\n\n\t\t\t\tn->search_col_list = $5;\n\t\t\t\tn->search_breadth_first = true;\n\t\t\t\tn->search_seq_column = $7;\n\t\t\t\tn->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t| /*EMPTY*/\n\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t$$ = NULL;\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\nopt_cycle_clause:\n\t\tCYCLE columnList SET ColId TO AexprConst DEFAULT AexprConst USING ColId\n\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\tCTECycleClause *n = makeNode(CTECycleClause);\n\n\t\t\t\tn->cycle_col_list = $2;\n\t\t\t\tn->cycle_mark_column = $4;\n\t\t\t\tn->cycle_mark_value = $6;\n\t\t\t\tn->cycle_mark_default = $8;\n\t\t\t\tn->cycle_path_column = $10;\n\t\t\t\tn->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t| CYCLE columnList SET ColId USING ColId\n\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\tCTECycleClause *n = makeNode(CTECycleClause);\n\n\t\t\t\tn->cycle_col_list = $2;\n\t\t\t\tn->cycle_mark_column = $4;\n\t\t\t\tn->cycle_mark_value = makeBoolAConst(true, -1);\n\t\t\t\tn->cycle_mark_default = makeBoolAConst(false, -1);\n\t\t\t\tn->cycle_path_column = $6;\n\t\t\t\tn->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t| /*EMPTY*/\n\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t$$ = NULL;\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\nopt_with_clause:\n\t\twith_clause\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t| /*EMPTY*/\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = NULL; }\n\t\t;\n\ninto_clause:\n\t\t\tINTO OptTempTableName\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeNode(IntoClause);\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->rel = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->colNames = NIL;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->options = NIL;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->onCommit = ONCOMMIT_NOOP;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->tableSpaceName = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->viewQuery = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->skipData = false;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| /*EMPTY*/\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = NULL; }\n\t\t;\n\n/*\n * Redundancy here is needed to avoid shift/reduce conflicts,\n * since TEMP is not a reserved word.  See also OptTemp.\n */\nOptTempTableName:\n\t\t\tTEMPORARY opt_table qualified_name\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = $3;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->relpersistence = RELPERSISTENCE_TEMP;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| TEMP opt_table qualified_name\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = $3;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->relpersistence = RELPERSISTENCE_TEMP;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| LOCAL TEMPORARY opt_table qualified_name\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = $4;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->relpersistence = RELPERSISTENCE_TEMP;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| LOCAL TEMP opt_table qualified_name\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = $4;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->relpersistence = RELPERSISTENCE_TEMP;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| GLOBAL TEMPORARY opt_table qualified_name\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tereport(WARNING,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t(errmsg(\"GLOBAL is deprecated in temporary table creation\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t parser_errposition(@1)));\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = $4;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->relpersistence = RELPERSISTENCE_TEMP;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| GLOBAL TEMP opt_table qualified_name\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tereport(WARNING,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t(errmsg(\"GLOBAL is deprecated in temporary table creation\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t parser_errposition(@1)));\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = $4;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->relpersistence = RELPERSISTENCE_TEMP;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| UNLOGGED opt_table qualified_name\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = $3;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->relpersistence = RELPERSISTENCE_UNLOGGED;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| TABLE qualified_name\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->relpersistence = RELPERSISTENCE_PERMANENT;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| qualified_name\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->relpersistence = RELPERSISTENCE_PERMANENT;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\nopt_table:\tTABLE\n\t\t\t| /*EMPTY*/\n\t\t;\n\nset_quantifier:\n\t\t\tALL\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = SET_QUANTIFIER_ALL; }\n\t\t\t| DISTINCT\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = SET_QUANTIFIER_DISTINCT; }\n\t\t\t| /*EMPTY*/\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = SET_QUANTIFIER_DEFAULT; }\n\t\t;\n\n/* We use (NIL) as a placeholder to indicate that all target expressions\n * should be placed in the DISTINCT list during parsetree analysis.\n */\ndistinct_clause:\n\t\t\tDISTINCT\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = list_make1(NIL); }\n\t\t\t| DISTINCT ON '(' expr_list ')'\t\t\t{ $$ = $4; }\n\t\t;\n\nopt_all_clause:\n\t\t\tALL\n\t\t\t| /*EMPTY*/\n\t\t;\n\nopt_distinct_clause:\n\t\t\tdistinct_clause\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| opt_all_clause\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = NIL; }\n\t\t;\n\nopt_sort_clause:\n\t\t\tsort_clause\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| /*EMPTY*/\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = NIL; }\n\t\t;\n\nsort_clause:\n\t\t\tORDER BY sortby_list\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $3; }\n\t\t;\n\nsortby_list:\n\t\t\tsortby\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = list_make1($1); }\n\t\t\t| sortby_list ',' sortby\t\t\t\t{ $$ = lappend($1, $3); }\n\t\t;\n\nsortby:\t\ta_expr USING qual_all_Op opt_nulls_order\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeNode(SortBy);\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->node = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->sortby_dir = SORTBY_USING;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->sortby_nulls = $4;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->useOp = $3;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->location = @3;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| a_expr opt_asc_desc opt_nulls_order\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeNode(SortBy);\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->node = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->sortby_dir = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->sortby_nulls = $3;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->useOp = NIL;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->location = -1;\t\t/* no operator */\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\n\nselect_limit:\n\t\t\tlimit_clause offset_clause\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\t($$)->limitOffset = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\t($$)->offsetLoc = @2;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| offset_clause limit_clause\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\t($$)->limitOffset = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\t($$)->offsetLoc = @1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| limit_clause\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = $1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| offset_clause\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tSelectLimit *n = (SelectLimit *) palloc(sizeof(SelectLimit));\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->limitOffset = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->limitCount = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->limitOption = LIMIT_OPTION_COUNT;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->offsetLoc = @1;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->countLoc = -1;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->optionLoc = -1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\nopt_select_limit:\n\t\t\tselect_limit\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| /* EMPTY */\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = NULL; }\n\t\t;\n\nlimit_clause:\n\t\t\tLIMIT select_limit_value\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tSelectLimit *n = (SelectLimit *) palloc(sizeof(SelectLimit));\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->limitOffset = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->limitCount = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->limitOption = LIMIT_OPTION_COUNT;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->offsetLoc = -1;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->countLoc = @1;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->optionLoc = -1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| LIMIT select_limit_value ',' select_offset_value\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t/* Disabled because it was too confusing, bjm 2002-02-18 */\n\t\t\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"LIMIT #,# syntax is not supported\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t errhint(\"Use separate LIMIT and OFFSET clauses.\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t parser_errposition(@1)));\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t/* SQL:2008 syntax */\n\t\t\t/* to avoid shift/reduce conflicts, handle the optional value with\n\t\t\t * a separate production rather than an opt_ expression.  The fact\n\t\t\t * that ONLY is fully reserved means that this way, we defer any\n\t\t\t * decision about what rule reduces ROW or ROWS to the point where\n\t\t\t * we can see the ONLY token in the lookahead slot.\n\t\t\t */\n\t\t\t| FETCH first_or_next select_fetch_first_value row_or_rows ONLY\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tSelectLimit *n = (SelectLimit *) palloc(sizeof(SelectLimit));\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->limitOffset = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->limitCount = $3;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->limitOption = LIMIT_OPTION_COUNT;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->offsetLoc = -1;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->countLoc = @1;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->optionLoc = -1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| FETCH first_or_next select_fetch_first_value row_or_rows WITH TIES\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tSelectLimit *n = (SelectLimit *) palloc(sizeof(SelectLimit));\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->limitOffset = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->limitCount = $3;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->limitOption = LIMIT_OPTION_WITH_TIES;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->offsetLoc = -1;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->countLoc = @1;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->optionLoc = @5;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| FETCH first_or_next row_or_rows ONLY\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tSelectLimit *n = (SelectLimit *) palloc(sizeof(SelectLimit));\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->limitOffset = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->limitCount = makeIntConst(1, -1);\n\t\t\t\t\tn->limitOption = LIMIT_OPTION_COUNT;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->offsetLoc = -1;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->countLoc = @1;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->optionLoc = -1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| FETCH first_or_next row_or_rows WITH TIES\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tSelectLimit *n = (SelectLimit *) palloc(sizeof(SelectLimit));\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->limitOffset = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->limitCount = makeIntConst(1, -1);\n\t\t\t\t\tn->limitOption = LIMIT_OPTION_WITH_TIES;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->offsetLoc = -1;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->countLoc = @1;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->optionLoc = @4;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\noffset_clause:\n\t\t\tOFFSET select_offset_value\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $2; }\n\t\t\t/* SQL:2008 syntax */\n\t\t\t| OFFSET select_fetch_first_value row_or_rows\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $2; }\n\t\t;\n\nselect_limit_value:\n\t\t\ta_expr\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| ALL\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t/* LIMIT ALL is represented as a NULL constant */\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeNullAConst(@1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\nselect_offset_value:\n\t\t\ta_expr\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t;\n\n/*\n * Allowing full expressions without parentheses causes various parsing\n * problems with the trailing ROW/ROWS key words.  SQL spec only calls for\n * <simple value specification>, which is either a literal or a parameter (but\n * an <SQL parameter reference> could be an identifier, bringing up conflicts\n * with ROW/ROWS). We solve this by leveraging the presence of ONLY (see above)\n * to determine whether the expression is missing rather than trying to make it\n * optional in this rule.\n *\n * c_expr covers almost all the spec-required cases (and more), but it doesn't\n * cover signed numeric literals, which are allowed by the spec. So we include\n * those here explicitly. We need FCONST as well as ICONST because values that\n * don't fit in the platform's \"long\", but do fit in bigint, should still be\n * accepted here. (This is possible in 64-bit Windows as well as all 32-bit\n * builds.)\n */\nselect_fetch_first_value:\n\t\t\tc_expr\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| '+' I_or_F_const\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, \"+\", NULL, $2, @1); }\n\t\t\t| '-' I_or_F_const\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = doNegate($2, @1); }\n\t\t;\n\nI_or_F_const:\n\t\t\tIconst\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = makeIntConst($1,@1); }\n\t\t\t| FCONST\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = makeFloatConst($1,@1); }\n\t\t;\n\n/* noise words */\nrow_or_rows: ROW\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = 0; }\n\t\t\t| ROWS\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = 0; }\n\t\t;\n\nfirst_or_next: FIRST_P\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = 0; }\n\t\t\t| NEXT\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = 0; }\n\t\t;\n\n\n/*\n * This syntax for group_clause tries to follow the spec quite closely.\n * However, the spec allows only column references, not expressions,\n * which introduces an ambiguity between implicit row constructors\n * (a,b) and lists of column references.\n *\n * We handle this by using the a_expr production for what the spec calls\n * <ordinary grouping set>, which in the spec represents either one column\n * reference or a parenthesized list of column references. Then, we check the\n * top node of the a_expr to see if it's an implicit RowExpr, and if so, just\n * grab and use the list, discarding the node. (this is done in parse analysis,\n * not here)\n *\n * (we abuse the row_format field of RowExpr to distinguish implicit and\n * explicit row constructors; it's debatable if anyone sanely wants to use them\n * in a group clause, but if they have a reason to, we make it possible.)\n *\n * Each item in the group_clause list is either an expression tree or a\n * GroupingSet node of some type.\n */\ngroup_clause:\n\t\t\tGROUP_P BY set_quantifier group_by_list\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tGroupClause *n = (GroupClause *) palloc(sizeof(GroupClause));\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->distinct = $3 == SET_QUANTIFIER_DISTINCT;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->list = $4;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| /*EMPTY*/\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tGroupClause *n = (GroupClause *) palloc(sizeof(GroupClause));\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->distinct = false;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->list = NIL;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\ngroup_by_list:\n\t\t\tgroup_by_item\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = list_make1($1); }\n\t\t\t| group_by_list ',' group_by_item\t\t{ $$ = lappend($1,$3); }\n\t\t;\n\ngroup_by_item:\n\t\t\ta_expr\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| empty_grouping_set\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| cube_clause\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| rollup_clause\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| grouping_sets_clause\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t;\n\nempty_grouping_set:\n\t\t\t'(' ')'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) makeGroupingSet(GROUPING_SET_EMPTY, NIL, @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\n/*\n * These hacks rely on setting precedence of CUBE and ROLLUP below that of '(',\n * so that they shift in these rules rather than reducing the conflicting\n * unreserved_keyword rule.\n */\n\nrollup_clause:\n\t\t\tROLLUP '(' expr_list ')'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) makeGroupingSet(GROUPING_SET_ROLLUP, $3, @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\ncube_clause:\n\t\t\tCUBE '(' expr_list ')'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) makeGroupingSet(GROUPING_SET_CUBE, $3, @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\ngrouping_sets_clause:\n\t\t\tGROUPING SETS '(' group_by_list ')'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) makeGroupingSet(GROUPING_SET_SETS, $4, @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\nhaving_clause:\n\t\t\tHAVING a_expr\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $2; }\n\t\t\t| /*EMPTY*/\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = NULL; }\n\t\t;\n\nfor_locking_clause:\n\t\t\tfor_locking_items\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| FOR READ ONLY\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = NIL; }\n\t\t;\n\nopt_for_locking_clause:\n\t\t\tfor_locking_clause\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| /* EMPTY */\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = NIL; }\n\t\t;\n\nfor_locking_items:\n\t\t\tfor_locking_item\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = list_make1($1); }\n\t\t\t| for_locking_items for_locking_item\t{ $$ = lappend($1, $2); }\n\t\t;\n\nfor_locking_item:\n\t\t\tfor_locking_strength locked_rels_list opt_nowait_or_skip\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tLockingClause *n = makeNode(LockingClause);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->lockedRels = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->strength = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->waitPolicy = $3;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\nfor_locking_strength:\n\t\t\tFOR UPDATE\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = LCS_FORUPDATE; }\n\t\t\t| FOR NO KEY UPDATE\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = LCS_FORNOKEYUPDATE; }\n\t\t\t| FOR SHARE\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = LCS_FORSHARE; }\n\t\t\t| FOR KEY SHARE\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = LCS_FORKEYSHARE; }\n\t\t;\n\nlocked_rels_list:\n\t\t\tOF qualified_name_list\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $2; }\n\t\t\t| /* EMPTY */\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = NIL; }\n\t\t;\n\n\n/*\n * We should allow ROW '(' expr_list ')' too, but that seems to require\n * making VALUES a fully reserved word, which will probably break more apps\n * than allowing the noise-word is worth.\n */\nvalues_clause:\n\t\t\tVALUES '(' expr_list ')'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tSelectStmt *n = makeNode(SelectStmt);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->valuesLists = list_make1($3);\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| values_clause ',' '(' expr_list ')'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tSelectStmt *n = (SelectStmt *) $1;\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->valuesLists = lappend(n->valuesLists, $4);\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\n\n/*****************************************************************************\n *\n *\tclauses common to all Optimizable Stmts:\n *\t\tfrom_clause\t\t- allow list of both JOIN expressions and table names\n *\t\twhere_clause\t- qualifications for joins or restrictions\n *\n *****************************************************************************/\n\nfrom_clause:\n\t\t\tFROM from_list\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $2; }\n\t\t\t| /*EMPTY*/\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = NIL; }\n\t\t;\n\nfrom_list:\n\t\t\ttable_ref\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = list_make1($1); }\n\t\t\t| from_list ',' table_ref\t\t\t\t{ $$ = lappend($1, $3); }\n\t\t;\n\n/*\n * table_ref is where an alias clause can be attached.\n */\ntable_ref:\trelation_expr opt_alias_clause\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$1->alias = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) $1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| relation_expr opt_alias_clause tablesample_clause\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tRangeTableSample *n = (RangeTableSample *) $3;\n\n\t\t\t\t\t$1->alias = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\t/* relation_expr goes inside the RangeTableSample node */\n\t\t\t\t\tn->relation = (Node *) $1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| func_table func_alias_clause\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tRangeFunction *n = (RangeFunction *) $1;\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->alias = linitial($2);\n\t\t\t\t\tn->coldeflist = lsecond($2);\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| LATERAL_P func_table func_alias_clause\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tRangeFunction *n = (RangeFunction *) $2;\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->lateral = true;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->alias = linitial($3);\n\t\t\t\t\tn->coldeflist = lsecond($3);\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| xmltable opt_alias_clause\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tRangeTableFunc *n = (RangeTableFunc *) $1;\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->alias = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| LATERAL_P xmltable opt_alias_clause\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tRangeTableFunc *n = (RangeTableFunc *) $2;\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->lateral = true;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->alias = $3;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| select_with_parens opt_alias_clause\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tRangeSubselect *n = makeNode(RangeSubselect);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->lateral = false;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->subquery = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->alias = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| LATERAL_P select_with_parens opt_alias_clause\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tRangeSubselect *n = makeNode(RangeSubselect);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->lateral = true;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->subquery = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->alias = $3;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| joined_table\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) $1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| '(' joined_table ')' alias_clause\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$2->alias = $4;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) $2;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| json_table opt_alias_clause\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tJsonTable  *jt = castNode(JsonTable, $1);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tjt->alias = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) jt;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| LATERAL_P json_table opt_alias_clause\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tJsonTable  *jt = castNode(JsonTable, $2);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tjt->alias = $3;\n\t\t\t\t\tjt->lateral = true;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) jt;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\n\n/*\n * It may seem silly to separate joined_table from table_ref, but there is\n * method in SQL's madness: if you don't do it this way you get reduce-\n * reduce conflicts, because it's not clear to the parser generator whether\n * to expect alias_clause after ')' or not.  For the same reason we must\n * treat 'JOIN' and 'join_type JOIN' separately, rather than allowing\n * join_type to expand to empty; if we try it, the parser generator can't\n * figure out when to reduce an empty join_type right after table_ref.\n *\n * Note that a CROSS JOIN is the same as an unqualified\n * INNER JOIN, and an INNER JOIN/ON has the same shape\n * but a qualification expression to limit membership.\n * A NATURAL JOIN implicitly matches column names between\n * tables and the shape is determined by which columns are\n * in common. We'll collect columns during the later transformations.\n */\n\njoined_table:\n\t\t\t'(' joined_table ')'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = $2;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| table_ref CROSS JOIN table_ref\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t/* CROSS JOIN is same as unqualified inner join */\n\t\t\t\t\tJoinExpr   *n = makeNode(JoinExpr);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->jointype = JOIN_INNER;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->isNatural = false;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->larg = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->rarg = $4;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->usingClause = NIL;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->join_using_alias = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->quals = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| table_ref join_type JOIN table_ref join_qual\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tJoinExpr   *n = makeNode(JoinExpr);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->jointype = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->isNatural = false;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->larg = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->rarg = $4;\n\t\t\t\t\tif ($5 != NULL && IsA($5, List))\n\t\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t\t /* USING clause */\n\t\t\t\t\t\tn->usingClause = linitial_node(List, castNode(List, $5));\n\t\t\t\t\t\tn->join_using_alias = lsecond_node(Alias, castNode(List, $5));\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\telse\n\t\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t\t/* ON clause */\n\t\t\t\t\t\tn->quals = $5;\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| table_ref JOIN table_ref join_qual\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t/* letting join_type reduce to empty doesn't work */\n\t\t\t\t\tJoinExpr   *n = makeNode(JoinExpr);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->jointype = JOIN_INNER;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->isNatural = false;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->larg = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->rarg = $3;\n\t\t\t\t\tif ($4 != NULL && IsA($4, List))\n\t\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t\t/* USING clause */\n\t\t\t\t\t\tn->usingClause = linitial_node(List, castNode(List, $4));\n\t\t\t\t\t\tn->join_using_alias = lsecond_node(Alias, castNode(List, $4));\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\telse\n\t\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t\t/* ON clause */\n\t\t\t\t\t\tn->quals = $4;\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| table_ref NATURAL join_type JOIN table_ref\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tJoinExpr   *n = makeNode(JoinExpr);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->jointype = $3;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->isNatural = true;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->larg = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->rarg = $5;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->usingClause = NIL; /* figure out which columns later... */\n\t\t\t\t\tn->join_using_alias = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->quals = NULL; /* fill later */\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| table_ref NATURAL JOIN table_ref\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t/* letting join_type reduce to empty doesn't work */\n\t\t\t\t\tJoinExpr   *n = makeNode(JoinExpr);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->jointype = JOIN_INNER;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->isNatural = true;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->larg = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->rarg = $4;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->usingClause = NIL; /* figure out which columns later... */\n\t\t\t\t\tn->join_using_alias = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->quals = NULL; /* fill later */\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\nalias_clause:\n\t\t\tAS ColId '(' name_list ')'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeNode(Alias);\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->aliasname = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->colnames = $4;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| AS ColId\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeNode(Alias);\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->aliasname = $2;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| ColId '(' name_list ')'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeNode(Alias);\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->aliasname = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->colnames = $3;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| ColId\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeNode(Alias);\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->aliasname = $1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\nopt_alias_clause: alias_clause\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| /*EMPTY*/\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = NULL; }\n\t\t;\n\n/*\n * The alias clause after JOIN ... USING only accepts the AS ColId spelling,\n * per SQL standard.  (The grammar could parse the other variants, but they\n * don't seem to be useful, and it might lead to parser problems in the\n * future.)\n */\nopt_alias_clause_for_join_using:\n\t\t\tAS ColId\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeNode(Alias);\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->aliasname = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\t/* the column name list will be inserted later */\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| /*EMPTY*/\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = NULL; }\n\t\t;\n\n/*\n * func_alias_clause can include both an Alias and a coldeflist, so we make it\n * return a 2-element list that gets disassembled by calling production.\n */\nfunc_alias_clause:\n\t\t\talias_clause\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = list_make2($1, NIL);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| AS '(' TableFuncElementList ')'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = list_make2(NULL, $3);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| AS ColId '(' TableFuncElementList ')'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tAlias\t   *a = makeNode(Alias);\n\n\t\t\t\t\ta->aliasname = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = list_make2(a, $4);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| ColId '(' TableFuncElementList ')'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tAlias\t   *a = makeNode(Alias);\n\n\t\t\t\t\ta->aliasname = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = list_make2(a, $3);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| /*EMPTY*/\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = list_make2(NULL, NIL);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\njoin_type:\tFULL opt_outer\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = JOIN_FULL; }\n\t\t\t| LEFT opt_outer\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = JOIN_LEFT; }\n\t\t\t| RIGHT opt_outer\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = JOIN_RIGHT; }\n\t\t\t| INNER_P\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = JOIN_INNER; }\n\t\t;\n\n/* OUTER is just noise... */\nopt_outer: OUTER_P\n\t\t\t| /*EMPTY*/\n\t\t;\n\n/* JOIN qualification clauses\n * Possibilities are:\n *\tUSING ( column list ) [ AS alias ]\n *\t\t\t\t\t\t  allows only unqualified column names,\n *\t\t\t\t\t\t  which must match between tables.\n *\tON expr allows more general qualifications.\n *\n * We return USING as a two-element List (the first item being a sub-List\n * of the common column names, and the second either an Alias item or NULL).\n * An ON-expr will not be a List, so it can be told apart that way.\n */\n\njoin_qual: USING '(' name_list ')' opt_alias_clause_for_join_using\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) list_make2($3, $5);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| ON a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = $2;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\n\nrelation_expr:\n\t\t\tqualified_name\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t/* inheritance query, implicitly */\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->inh = true;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->alias = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| extended_relation_expr\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = $1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\nextended_relation_expr:\n\t\t\tqualified_name '*'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t/* inheritance query, explicitly */\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->inh = true;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->alias = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| ONLY qualified_name\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t/* no inheritance */\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->inh = false;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->alias = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| ONLY '(' qualified_name ')'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t/* no inheritance, SQL99-style syntax */\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = $3;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->inh = false;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->alias = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\n\nrelation_expr_list:\n\t\t\trelation_expr\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = list_make1($1); }\n\t\t\t| relation_expr_list ',' relation_expr\t{ $$ = lappend($1, $3); }\n\t\t;\n\n\n/*\n * Given \"UPDATE foo set set ...\", we have to decide without looking any\n * further ahead whether the first \"set\" is an alias or the UPDATE's SET\n * keyword.  Since \"set\" is allowed as a column name both interpretations\n * are feasible.  We resolve the shift/reduce conflict by giving the first\n * relation_expr_opt_alias production a higher precedence than the SET token\n * has, causing the parser to prefer to reduce, in effect assuming that the\n * SET is not an alias.\n */\nrelation_expr_opt_alias: relation_expr\t\t\t\t\t%prec UMINUS\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = $1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| relation_expr ColId\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tAlias\t   *alias = makeNode(Alias);\n\n\t\t\t\t\talias->aliasname = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\t$1->alias = alias;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = $1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| relation_expr AS ColId\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tAlias\t   *alias = makeNode(Alias);\n\n\t\t\t\t\talias->aliasname = $3;\n\t\t\t\t\t$1->alias = alias;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = $1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\n/*\n * TABLESAMPLE decoration in a FROM item\n */\ntablesample_clause:\n\t\t\tTABLESAMPLE func_name '(' expr_list ')' opt_repeatable_clause\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tRangeTableSample *n = makeNode(RangeTableSample);\n\n\t\t\t\t\t/* n->relation will be filled in later */\n\t\t\t\t\tn->method = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->args = $4;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->repeatable = $6;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->location = @2;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\nopt_repeatable_clause:\n\t\t\tREPEATABLE '(' a_expr ')'\t{ $$ = (Node *) $3; }\n\t\t\t| /*EMPTY*/\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = NULL; }\n\t\t;\n\n/*\n * func_table represents a function invocation in a FROM list. It can be\n * a plain function call, like \"foo(...)\", or a ROWS FROM expression with\n * one or more function calls, \"ROWS FROM (foo(...), bar(...))\",\n * optionally with WITH ORDINALITY attached.\n * In the ROWS FROM syntax, a column definition list can be given for each\n * function, for example:\n *     ROWS FROM (foo() AS (foo_res_a text, foo_res_b text),\n *                bar() AS (bar_res_a text, bar_res_b text))\n * It's also possible to attach a column definition list to the RangeFunction\n * as a whole, but that's handled by the table_ref production.\n */\nfunc_table: func_expr_windowless opt_ordinality\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tRangeFunction *n = makeNode(RangeFunction);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->lateral = false;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->ordinality = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->is_rowsfrom = false;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->functions = list_make1(list_make2($1, NIL));\n\t\t\t\t\t/* alias and coldeflist are set by table_ref production */\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| ROWS FROM '(' rowsfrom_list ')' opt_ordinality\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tRangeFunction *n = makeNode(RangeFunction);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->lateral = false;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->ordinality = $6;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->is_rowsfrom = true;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->functions = $4;\n\t\t\t\t\t/* alias and coldeflist are set by table_ref production */\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\nrowsfrom_item: func_expr_windowless opt_col_def_list\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = list_make2($1, $2); }\n\t\t;\n\nrowsfrom_list:\n\t\t\trowsfrom_item\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = list_make1($1); }\n\t\t\t| rowsfrom_list ',' rowsfrom_item\t{ $$ = lappend($1, $3); }\n\t\t;\n\nopt_col_def_list: AS '(' TableFuncElementList ')'\t{ $$ = $3; }\n\t\t\t| /*EMPTY*/\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = NIL; }\n\t\t;\n\nopt_ordinality: WITH_LA ORDINALITY\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = true; }\n\t\t\t| /*EMPTY*/\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = false; }\n\t\t;\n\n\nwhere_clause:\n\t\t\tWHERE a_expr\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $2; }\n\t\t\t| /*EMPTY*/\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = NULL; }\n\t\t;\n\n/* variant for UPDATE and DELETE */\nwhere_or_current_clause:\n\t\t\tWHERE a_expr\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $2; }\n\t\t\t| WHERE CURRENT_P OF cursor_name\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tCurrentOfExpr *n = makeNode(CurrentOfExpr);\n\n\t\t\t\t\t/* cvarno is filled in by parse analysis */\n\t\t\t\t\tn->cursor_name = $4;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->cursor_param = 0;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| /*EMPTY*/\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = NULL; }\n\t\t;\n\n\nOptTableFuncElementList:\n\t\t\tTableFuncElementList\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| /*EMPTY*/\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = NIL; }\n\t\t;\n\nTableFuncElementList:\n\t\t\tTableFuncElement\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = list_make1($1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| TableFuncElementList ',' TableFuncElement\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = lappend($1, $3);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\nTableFuncElement:\tColId Typename opt_collate_clause\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tColumnDef *n = makeNode(ColumnDef);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->colname = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->typeName = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->inhcount = 0;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->is_local = true;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->is_not_null = false;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->is_from_type = false;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->storage = 0;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->raw_default = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->cooked_default = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->collClause = (CollateClause *) $3;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->collOid = InvalidOid;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->constraints = NIL;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\n/*\n * XMLTABLE\n */\nxmltable:\n\t\t\tXMLTABLE '(' c_expr xmlexists_argument COLUMNS xmltable_column_list ')'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tRangeTableFunc *n = makeNode(RangeTableFunc);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->rowexpr = $3;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->docexpr = $4;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->columns = $6;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->namespaces = NIL;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| XMLTABLE '(' XMLNAMESPACES '(' xml_namespace_list ')' ','\n\t\t\t\tc_expr xmlexists_argument COLUMNS xmltable_column_list ')'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tRangeTableFunc *n = makeNode(RangeTableFunc);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->rowexpr = $8;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->docexpr = $9;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->columns = $11;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->namespaces = $5;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\nxmltable_column_list: xmltable_column_el\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = list_make1($1); }\n\t\t\t| xmltable_column_list ',' xmltable_column_el\t{ $$ = lappend($1, $3); }\n\t\t;\n\nxmltable_column_el:\n\t\t\tColId Typename\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tRangeTableFuncCol *fc = makeNode(RangeTableFuncCol);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tfc->colname = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\tfc->for_ordinality = false;\n\t\t\t\t\tfc->typeName = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\tfc->is_not_null = false;\n\t\t\t\t\tfc->colexpr = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\tfc->coldefexpr = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\tfc->location = @1;\n\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) fc;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| ColId Typename xmltable_column_option_list\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tRangeTableFuncCol *fc = makeNode(RangeTableFuncCol);\n\t\t\t\t\tListCell   *option;\n\t\t\t\t\tbool\t\tnullability_seen = false;\n\n\t\t\t\t\tfc->colname = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\tfc->typeName = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\tfc->for_ordinality = false;\n\t\t\t\t\tfc->is_not_null = false;\n\t\t\t\t\tfc->colexpr = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\tfc->coldefexpr = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\tfc->location = @1;\n\n\t\t\t\t\tforeach(option, $3)\n\t\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t\tDefElem   *defel = (DefElem *) lfirst(option);\n\n\t\t\t\t\t\tif (strcmp(defel->defname, \"default\") == 0)\n\t\t\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tif (fc->coldefexpr != NULL)\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"only one DEFAULT value is allowed\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t parser_errposition(defel->location)));\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tfc->coldefexpr = defel->arg;\n\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t\telse if (strcmp(defel->defname, \"path\") == 0)\n\t\t\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tif (fc->colexpr != NULL)\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"only one PATH value per column is allowed\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t parser_errposition(defel->location)));\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tfc->colexpr = defel->arg;\n\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t\telse if (strcmp(defel->defname, \"__pg__is_not_null\") == 0)\n\t\t\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tif (nullability_seen)\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"conflicting or redundant NULL / NOT NULL declarations for column \\\"%s\\\"\", fc->colname),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t parser_errposition(defel->location)));\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tfc->is_not_null = boolVal(defel->arg);\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tnullability_seen = true;\n\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t\telse\n\t\t\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"unrecognized column option \\\"%s\\\"\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tdefel->defname),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t parser_errposition(defel->location)));\n\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) fc;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| ColId FOR ORDINALITY\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tRangeTableFuncCol *fc = makeNode(RangeTableFuncCol);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tfc->colname = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\tfc->for_ordinality = true;\n\t\t\t\t\t/* other fields are ignored, initialized by makeNode */\n\t\t\t\t\tfc->location = @1;\n\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) fc;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\nxmltable_column_option_list:\n\t\t\txmltable_column_option_el\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = list_make1($1); }\n\t\t\t| xmltable_column_option_list xmltable_column_option_el\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = lappend($1, $2); }\n\t\t;\n\nxmltable_column_option_el:\n\t\t\tIDENT b_expr\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tif (strcmp($1, \"__pg__is_not_null\") == 0)\n\t\t\t\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"option name \\\"%s\\\" cannot be used in XMLTABLE\", $1),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t parser_errposition(@1)));\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeDefElem($1, $2, @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| DEFAULT b_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = makeDefElem(\"default\", $2, @1); }\n\t\t\t| NOT NULL_P\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = makeDefElem(\"__pg__is_not_null\", (Node *) makeBoolean(true), @1); }\n\t\t\t| NULL_P\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = makeDefElem(\"__pg__is_not_null\", (Node *) makeBoolean(false), @1); }\n\t\t\t| PATH b_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = makeDefElem(\"path\", $2, @1); }\n\t\t;\n\nxml_namespace_list:\n\t\t\txml_namespace_el\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = list_make1($1); }\n\t\t\t| xml_namespace_list ',' xml_namespace_el\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = lappend($1, $3); }\n\t\t;\n\nxml_namespace_el:\n\t\t\tb_expr AS ColLabel\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeNode(ResTarget);\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->name = $3;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->indirection = NIL;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->val = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| DEFAULT b_expr\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeNode(ResTarget);\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->name = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->indirection = NIL;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->val = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\njson_table:\n\t\t\tJSON_TABLE '('\n\t\t\t\tjson_value_expr ',' a_expr json_table_path_name_opt\n\t\t\t\tjson_passing_clause_opt\n\t\t\t\tCOLUMNS '(' json_table_column_definition_list ')'\n\t\t\t\tjson_on_error_clause_opt\n\t\t\t')'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tJsonTable *n = makeNode(JsonTable);\n\t\t\t\t\tchar\t  *pathstring;\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->context_item = (JsonValueExpr *) $3;\n\t\t\t\t\tif (!IsA($5, A_Const) ||\n\t\t\t\t\t\tcastNode(A_Const, $5)->val.node.type != T_String)\n\t\t\t\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\terrcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\terrmsg(\"only string constants are supported in JSON_TABLE path specification\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tparser_errposition(@5));\n\t\t\t\t\tpathstring = castNode(A_Const, $5)->val.sval.sval;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->pathspec = makeJsonTablePathSpec(pathstring, $6, @5, @6);\n\t\t\t\t\tn->passing = $7;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->columns = $10;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->on_error = (JsonBehavior *) $12;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\njson_table_path_name_opt:\n\t\t\tAS name\t\t\t{ $$ = $2; }\n\t\t\t| /* empty */\t{ $$ = NULL; }\n\t\t;\n\njson_table_column_definition_list:\n\t\t\tjson_table_column_definition\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = list_make1($1); }\n\t\t\t| json_table_column_definition_list ',' json_table_column_definition\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = lappend($1, $3); }\n\t\t;\n\njson_table_column_definition:\n\t\t\tColId FOR ORDINALITY\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tJsonTableColumn *n = makeNode(JsonTableColumn);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->coltype = JTC_FOR_ORDINALITY;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->name = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| ColId Typename\n\t\t\t\tjson_table_column_path_clause_opt\n\t\t\t\tjson_wrapper_behavior\n\t\t\t\tjson_quotes_clause_opt\n\t\t\t\tjson_behavior_clause_opt\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tJsonTableColumn *n = makeNode(JsonTableColumn);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->coltype = JTC_REGULAR;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->name = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->typeName = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->format = makeJsonFormat(JS_FORMAT_DEFAULT, JS_ENC_DEFAULT, -1);\n\t\t\t\t\tn->pathspec = (JsonTablePathSpec *) $3;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->wrapper = $4;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->quotes = $5;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->on_empty = (JsonBehavior *) linitial($6);\n\t\t\t\t\tn->on_error = (JsonBehavior *) lsecond($6);\n\t\t\t\t\tn->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| ColId Typename json_format_clause\n\t\t\t\tjson_table_column_path_clause_opt\n\t\t\t\tjson_wrapper_behavior\n\t\t\t\tjson_quotes_clause_opt\n\t\t\t\tjson_behavior_clause_opt\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tJsonTableColumn *n = makeNode(JsonTableColumn);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->coltype = JTC_FORMATTED;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->name = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->typeName = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->format = (JsonFormat *) $3;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->pathspec = (JsonTablePathSpec *) $4;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->wrapper = $5;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->quotes = $6;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->on_empty = (JsonBehavior *) linitial($7);\n\t\t\t\t\tn->on_error = (JsonBehavior *) lsecond($7);\n\t\t\t\t\tn->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| ColId Typename\n\t\t\t\tEXISTS json_table_column_path_clause_opt\n\t\t\t\tjson_on_error_clause_opt\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tJsonTableColumn *n = makeNode(JsonTableColumn);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->coltype = JTC_EXISTS;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->name = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->typeName = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->format = makeJsonFormat(JS_FORMAT_DEFAULT, JS_ENC_DEFAULT, -1);\n\t\t\t\t\tn->wrapper = JSW_NONE;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->quotes = JS_QUOTES_UNSPEC;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->pathspec = (JsonTablePathSpec *) $4;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->on_empty = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->on_error = (JsonBehavior *) $5;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| NESTED path_opt Sconst\n\t\t\t\tCOLUMNS '(' json_table_column_definition_list ')'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tJsonTableColumn *n = makeNode(JsonTableColumn);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->coltype = JTC_NESTED;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->pathspec = (JsonTablePathSpec *)\n\t\t\t\t\t\tmakeJsonTablePathSpec($3, NULL, @3, -1);\n\t\t\t\t\tn->columns = $6;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| NESTED path_opt Sconst AS name\n\t\t\t\tCOLUMNS '(' json_table_column_definition_list ')'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tJsonTableColumn *n = makeNode(JsonTableColumn);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->coltype = JTC_NESTED;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->pathspec = (JsonTablePathSpec *)\n\t\t\t\t\t\tmakeJsonTablePathSpec($3, $5, @3, @5);\n\t\t\t\t\tn->columns = $8;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\npath_opt:\n\t\t\tPATH\n\t\t\t| /* EMPTY */\n\t\t;\n\njson_table_column_path_clause_opt:\n\t\t\tPATH Sconst\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = (Node *) makeJsonTablePathSpec($2, NULL, @2, -1); }\n\t\t\t| /* EMPTY */\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = NULL; }\n\t\t;\n\n/*****************************************************************************\n *\n *\tType syntax\n *\t\tSQL introduces a large amount of type-specific syntax.\n *\t\tDefine individual clauses to handle these cases, and use\n *\t\t the generic case to handle regular type-extensible Postgres syntax.\n *\t\t- thomas 1997-10-10\n *\n *****************************************************************************/\n\nTypename:\tSimpleTypename opt_array_bounds\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->arrayBounds = $2;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| SETOF SimpleTypename opt_array_bounds\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->arrayBounds = $3;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->setof = true;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t/* SQL standard syntax, currently only one-dimensional */\n\t\t\t| SimpleTypename ARRAY '[' Iconst ']'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->arrayBounds = list_make1(makeInteger($4));\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| SETOF SimpleTypename ARRAY '[' Iconst ']'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->arrayBounds = list_make1(makeInteger($5));\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->setof = true;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| SimpleTypename ARRAY\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->arrayBounds = list_make1(makeInteger(-1));\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| SETOF SimpleTypename ARRAY\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->arrayBounds = list_make1(makeInteger(-1));\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->setof = true;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\nopt_array_bounds:\n\t\t\topt_array_bounds '[' ']'\n\t\t\t\t\t{  $$ = lappend($1, makeInteger(-1)); }\n\t\t\t| opt_array_bounds '[' Iconst ']'\n\t\t\t\t\t{  $$ = lappend($1, makeInteger($3)); }\n\t\t\t| /*EMPTY*/\n\t\t\t\t\t{  $$ = NIL; }\n\t\t;\n\nSimpleTypename:\n\t\t\tGenericType\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| Numeric\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| Bit\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| Character\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| ConstDatetime\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| ConstInterval opt_interval\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->typmods = $2;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| ConstInterval '(' Iconst ')'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->typmods = list_make2(makeIntConst(INTERVAL_FULL_RANGE, -1),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t makeIntConst($3, @3));\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| JsonType\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t;\n\n/* We have a separate ConstTypename to allow defaulting fixed-length\n * types such as CHAR() and BIT() to an unspecified length.\n * SQL9x requires that these default to a length of one, but this\n * makes no sense for constructs like CHAR 'hi' and BIT '0101',\n * where there is an obvious better choice to make.\n * Note that ConstInterval is not included here since it must\n * be pushed up higher in the rules to accommodate the postfix\n * options (e.g. INTERVAL '1' YEAR). Likewise, we have to handle\n * the generic-type-name case in AexprConst to avoid premature\n * reduce/reduce conflicts against function names.\n */\nConstTypename:\n\t\t\tNumeric\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| ConstBit\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| ConstCharacter\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| ConstDatetime\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| JsonType\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t;\n\n/*\n * GenericType covers all type names that don't have special syntax mandated\n * by the standard, including qualified names.  We also allow type modifiers.\n * To avoid parsing conflicts against function invocations, the modifiers\n * have to be shown as expr_list here, but parse analysis will only accept\n * constants for them.\n */\nGenericType:\n\t\t\ttype_function_name opt_type_modifiers\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeTypeName($1);\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->typmods = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| type_function_name attrs opt_type_modifiers\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeTypeNameFromNameList(lcons(makeString($1), $2));\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->typmods = $3;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\nopt_type_modifiers: '(' expr_list ')'\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $2; }\n\t\t\t\t\t| /* EMPTY */\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = NIL; }\n\t\t;\n\n/*\n * SQL numeric data types\n */\nNumeric:\tINT_P\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = SystemTypeName(\"int4\");\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| INTEGER\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = SystemTypeName(\"int4\");\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| SMALLINT\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = SystemTypeName(\"int2\");\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| BIGINT\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = SystemTypeName(\"int8\");\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| REAL\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = SystemTypeName(\"float4\");\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| FLOAT_P opt_float\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| DOUBLE_P PRECISION\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = SystemTypeName(\"float8\");\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| DECIMAL_P opt_type_modifiers\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = SystemTypeName(\"numeric\");\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->typmods = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| DEC opt_type_modifiers\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = SystemTypeName(\"numeric\");\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->typmods = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| NUMERIC opt_type_modifiers\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = SystemTypeName(\"numeric\");\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->typmods = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| BOOLEAN_P\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = SystemTypeName(\"bool\");\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\nopt_float:\t'(' Iconst ')'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t/*\n\t\t\t\t\t * Check FLOAT() precision limits assuming IEEE floating\n\t\t\t\t\t * types - thomas 1997-09-18\n\t\t\t\t\t */\n\t\t\t\t\tif ($2 < 1)\n\t\t\t\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"precision for type float must be at least 1 bit\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t parser_errposition(@2)));\n\t\t\t\t\telse if ($2 <= 24)\n\t\t\t\t\t\t$$ = SystemTypeName(\"float4\");\n\t\t\t\t\telse if ($2 <= 53)\n\t\t\t\t\t\t$$ = SystemTypeName(\"float8\");\n\t\t\t\t\telse\n\t\t\t\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"precision for type float must be less than 54 bits\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t parser_errposition(@2)));\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| /*EMPTY*/\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = SystemTypeName(\"float8\");\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\n/*\n * SQL bit-field data types\n * The following implements BIT() and BIT VARYING().\n */\nBit:\t\tBitWithLength\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = $1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| BitWithoutLength\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = $1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\n/* ConstBit is like Bit except \"BIT\" defaults to unspecified length */\n/* See notes for ConstCharacter, which addresses same issue for \"CHAR\" */\nConstBit:\tBitWithLength\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = $1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| BitWithoutLength\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->typmods = NIL;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\nBitWithLength:\n\t\t\tBIT opt_varying '(' expr_list ')'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tchar *typname;\n\n\t\t\t\t\ttypname = $2 ? \"varbit\" : \"bit\";\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = SystemTypeName(typname);\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->typmods = $4;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\nBitWithoutLength:\n\t\t\tBIT opt_varying\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t/* bit defaults to bit(1), varbit to no limit */\n\t\t\t\t\tif ($2)\n\t\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t\t$$ = SystemTypeName(\"varbit\");\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\telse\n\t\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t\t$$ = SystemTypeName(\"bit\");\n\t\t\t\t\t\t$$->typmods = list_make1(makeIntConst(1, -1));\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\n\n/*\n * SQL character data types\n * The following implements CHAR() and VARCHAR().\n */\nCharacter:  CharacterWithLength\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = $1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| CharacterWithoutLength\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = $1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\nConstCharacter:  CharacterWithLength\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = $1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| CharacterWithoutLength\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t/* Length was not specified so allow to be unrestricted.\n\t\t\t\t\t * This handles problems with fixed-length (bpchar) strings\n\t\t\t\t\t * which in column definitions must default to a length\n\t\t\t\t\t * of one, but should not be constrained if the length\n\t\t\t\t\t * was not specified.\n\t\t\t\t\t */\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->typmods = NIL;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\nCharacterWithLength:  character '(' Iconst ')'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = SystemTypeName($1);\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->typmods = list_make1(makeIntConst($3, @3));\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\nCharacterWithoutLength:\t character\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = SystemTypeName($1);\n\t\t\t\t\t/* char defaults to char(1), varchar to no limit */\n\t\t\t\t\tif (strcmp($1, \"bpchar\") == 0)\n\t\t\t\t\t\t$$->typmods = list_make1(makeIntConst(1, -1));\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\ncharacter:\tCHARACTER opt_varying\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $2 ? \"varchar\": \"bpchar\"; }\n\t\t\t| CHAR_P opt_varying\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $2 ? \"varchar\": \"bpchar\"; }\n\t\t\t| VARCHAR\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = \"varchar\"; }\n\t\t\t| NATIONAL CHARACTER opt_varying\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $3 ? \"varchar\": \"bpchar\"; }\n\t\t\t| NATIONAL CHAR_P opt_varying\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $3 ? \"varchar\": \"bpchar\"; }\n\t\t\t| NCHAR opt_varying\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $2 ? \"varchar\": \"bpchar\"; }\n\t\t;\n\nopt_varying:\n\t\t\tVARYING\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = true; }\n\t\t\t| /*EMPTY*/\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = false; }\n\t\t;\n\n/*\n * SQL date/time types\n */\nConstDatetime:\n\t\t\tTIMESTAMP '(' Iconst ')' opt_timezone\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tif ($5)\n\t\t\t\t\t\t$$ = SystemTypeName(\"timestamptz\");\n\t\t\t\t\telse\n\t\t\t\t\t\t$$ = SystemTypeName(\"timestamp\");\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->typmods = list_make1(makeIntConst($3, @3));\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| TIMESTAMP opt_timezone\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tif ($2)\n\t\t\t\t\t\t$$ = SystemTypeName(\"timestamptz\");\n\t\t\t\t\telse\n\t\t\t\t\t\t$$ = SystemTypeName(\"timestamp\");\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| TIME '(' Iconst ')' opt_timezone\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tif ($5)\n\t\t\t\t\t\t$$ = SystemTypeName(\"timetz\");\n\t\t\t\t\telse\n\t\t\t\t\t\t$$ = SystemTypeName(\"time\");\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->typmods = list_make1(makeIntConst($3, @3));\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| TIME opt_timezone\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tif ($2)\n\t\t\t\t\t\t$$ = SystemTypeName(\"timetz\");\n\t\t\t\t\telse\n\t\t\t\t\t\t$$ = SystemTypeName(\"time\");\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\nConstInterval:\n\t\t\tINTERVAL\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = SystemTypeName(\"interval\");\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\nopt_timezone:\n\t\t\tWITH_LA TIME ZONE\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = true; }\n\t\t\t| WITHOUT_LA TIME ZONE\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = false; }\n\t\t\t| /*EMPTY*/\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = false; }\n\t\t;\n\nopt_interval:\n\t\t\tYEAR_P\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = list_make1(makeIntConst(INTERVAL_MASK(YEAR), @1)); }\n\t\t\t| MONTH_P\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = list_make1(makeIntConst(INTERVAL_MASK(MONTH), @1)); }\n\t\t\t| DAY_P\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = list_make1(makeIntConst(INTERVAL_MASK(DAY), @1)); }\n\t\t\t| HOUR_P\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = list_make1(makeIntConst(INTERVAL_MASK(HOUR), @1)); }\n\t\t\t| MINUTE_P\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = list_make1(makeIntConst(INTERVAL_MASK(MINUTE), @1)); }\n\t\t\t| interval_second\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| YEAR_P TO MONTH_P\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = list_make1(makeIntConst(INTERVAL_MASK(YEAR) |\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t INTERVAL_MASK(MONTH), @1));\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| DAY_P TO HOUR_P\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = list_make1(makeIntConst(INTERVAL_MASK(DAY) |\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t INTERVAL_MASK(HOUR), @1));\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| DAY_P TO MINUTE_P\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = list_make1(makeIntConst(INTERVAL_MASK(DAY) |\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t INTERVAL_MASK(HOUR) |\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t INTERVAL_MASK(MINUTE), @1));\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| DAY_P TO interval_second\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = $3;\n\t\t\t\t\tlinitial($$) = makeIntConst(INTERVAL_MASK(DAY) |\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tINTERVAL_MASK(HOUR) |\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tINTERVAL_MASK(MINUTE) INTERVAL_MASK(MINUTE), @1));\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| HOUR_P TO interval_second\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = $3;\n\t\t\t\t\tlinitial($$) = makeIntConst(INTERVAL_MASK(HOUR)
