}\n\t\t;\n\ninterval_second:\n\t\t\tSECOND_P\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = list_make1(makeIntConst(INTERVAL_MASK(SECOND), @1));\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| SECOND_P '(' Iconst ')'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = list_make2(makeIntConst(INTERVAL_MASK(SECOND), @1),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tmakeIntConst($3, @3));\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\nJsonType:\n\t\t\tJSON\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = SystemTypeName(\"json\");\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\n/*****************************************************************************\n *\n *\texpression grammar\n *\n *****************************************************************************/\n\n/*\n * General expressions\n * This is the heart of the expression syntax.\n *\n * We have two expression types: a_expr is the unrestricted kind, and\n * b_expr is a subset that must be used in some places to avoid shift/reduce\n * conflicts.  For example, we can't do BETWEEN as \"BETWEEN a_expr AND a_expr\"\n * because that use of AND conflicts with AND as a boolean operator.  So,\n * b_expr is used in BETWEEN and we remove boolean keywords from b_expr.\n *\n * Note that '(' a_expr ')' is a b_expr, so an unrestricted expression can\n * always be used by surrounding it with parens.\n *\n * c_expr is all the productions that are common to a_expr and b_expr;\n * it's factored out just to eliminate redundant coding.\n *\n * Be careful of productions involving more than one terminal token.\n * By default, bison will assign such productions the precedence of their\n * last terminal, but in nearly all cases you want it to be the precedence\n * of the first terminal instead; otherwise you will not get the behavior\n * you expect!  So we use %prec annotations freely to set precedences.\n */\na_expr:\t\tc_expr\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| a_expr TYPECAST Typename\n\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = makeTypeCast($1, $3, @2); }\n\t\t\t| a_expr COLLATE any_name\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tCollateClause *n = makeNode(CollateClause);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->arg = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->collname = $3;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->location = @2;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| a_expr AT TIME ZONE a_expr\t\t\t%prec AT\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) makeFuncCall(SystemFuncName(\"timezone\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   list_make2($5, $1),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   COERCE_SQL_SYNTAX,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   @2);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| a_expr AT LOCAL\t\t\t\t\t\t%prec AT\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) makeFuncCall(SystemFuncName(\"timezone\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   list_make1($1),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   COERCE_SQL_SYNTAX,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   -1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t/*\n\t\t * These operators must be called out explicitly in order to make use\n\t\t * of bison's automatic operator-precedence handling.  All other\n\t\t * operator names are handled by the generic productions using \"Op\",\n\t\t * below; and all those operators will have the same precedence.\n\t\t *\n\t\t * If you add more explicitly-known operators, be sure to add them\n\t\t * also to b_expr and to the MathOp list below.\n\t\t */\n\t\t\t| '+' a_expr\t\t\t\t\t%prec UMINUS\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, \"+\", NULL, $2, @1); }\n\t\t\t| '-' a_expr\t\t\t\t\t%prec UMINUS\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = doNegate($2, @1); }\n\t\t\t| a_expr '+' a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, \"+\", $1, $3, @2); }\n\t\t\t| a_expr '-' a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, \"-\", $1, $3, @2); }\n\t\t\t| a_expr '*' a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, \"*\", $1, $3, @2); }\n\t\t\t| a_expr '/' a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, \"/\", $1, $3, @2); }\n\t\t\t| a_expr '%' a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, \"%\", $1, $3, @2); }\n\t\t\t| a_expr '^' a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, \"^\", $1, $3, @2); }\n\t\t\t| a_expr '<' a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, \"<\", $1, $3, @2); }\n\t\t\t| a_expr '>' a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, \">\", $1, $3, @2); }\n\t\t\t| a_expr '=' a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, \"=\", $1, $3, @2); }\n\t\t\t| a_expr LESS_EQUALS a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, \"<=\", $1, $3, @2); }\n\t\t\t| a_expr GREATER_EQUALS a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, \">=\", $1, $3, @2); }\n\t\t\t| a_expr NOT_EQUALS a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, \"<>\", $1, $3, @2); }\n\n\t\t\t| a_expr qual_Op a_expr\t\t\t\t%prec Op\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = (Node *) makeA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, $2, $1, $3, @2); }\n\t\t\t| qual_Op a_expr\t\t\t\t\t%prec Op\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = (Node *) makeA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, $1, NULL, $2, @1); }\n\n\t\t\t| a_expr AND a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = makeAndExpr($1, $3, @2); }\n\t\t\t| a_expr OR a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = makeOrExpr($1, $3, @2); }\n\t\t\t| NOT a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = makeNotExpr($2, @1); }\n\t\t\t| NOT_LA a_expr\t\t\t\t\t\t%prec NOT\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = makeNotExpr($2, @1); }\n\n\t\t\t| a_expr LIKE a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_LIKE, \"~~\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   $1, $3, @2);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| a_expr LIKE a_expr ESCAPE a_expr\t\t\t\t\t%prec LIKE\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tFuncCall   *n = makeFuncCall(SystemFuncName(\"like_escape\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t list_make2($3, $5),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t COERCE_EXPLICIT_CALL,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t @2);\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_LIKE, \"~~\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   $1, (Node *) n, @2);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| a_expr NOT_LA LIKE a_expr\t\t\t\t\t\t\t%prec NOT_LA\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_LIKE, \"!~~\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   $1, $4, @2);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| a_expr NOT_LA LIKE a_expr ESCAPE a_expr\t\t\t%prec NOT_LA\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tFuncCall   *n = makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_LIKE, \"!~~\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   $1, (Node *) n, @2);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| a_expr ILIKE a_expr\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_ILIKE, \"~~*\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   $1, $3, @2);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| a_expr ILIKE a_expr ESCAPE a_expr\t\t\t\t\t%prec ILIKE\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tFuncCall   *n = makeFuncCall(SystemFuncName(\"like_escape\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t list_make2($3, $5),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t COERCE_EXPLICIT_CALL,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t @2);\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_ILIKE, \"~~*\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   $1, (Node *) n, @2);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| a_expr NOT_LA ILIKE a_expr\t\t\t\t\t\t%prec NOT_LA\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_SIMILAR, \"~\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   $1, (Node *) n, @2);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| a_expr SIMILAR TO a_expr ESCAPE a_expr\t\t\t%prec SIMILAR\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tFuncCall @2);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t/* NullTest clause\n\t\t\t * Define SQL-style Null test clause.\n\t\t\t * Allow two forms described in the standard:\n\t\t\t *\ta IS NULL\n\t\t\t *\ta IS NOT NULL\n\t\t\t * Allow two SQL extensions\n\t\t\t *\ta ISNULL\n\t\t\t *\ta NOTNULL\n\t\t\t */\n\t\t\t| a_expr IS NULL_P\t\t\t\t\t\t\t%prec IS\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tNullTest   *n = makeNode(NullTest);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->arg = (Expr *) $1;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->nulltesttype = IS_NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->location = @2;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| a_expr ISNULL\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tNullTest   *n = makeNode(NullTest);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->arg = (Expr *) $1;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->nulltesttype = IS_NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->location = @2;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| a_expr IS NOT NULL_P\t\t\t\t\t\t%prec IS\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tNullTest   *n = makeNode(NullTest);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->arg = (Expr *) $1;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->nulltesttype = IS_NOT_NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->location = @2;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| a_expr NOTNULL\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tNullTest   *n = makeNode(NullTest);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->arg = (Expr *) $1;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->nulltesttype = IS_NOT_NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->location = @2;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| row OVERLAPS row\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tif (list_length($1) != 2)\n\t\t\t\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"wrong number of parameters on left side of OVERLAPS expression\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t makeNode(BooleanTest);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tb->arg = (Expr *) $1;\n\t\t\t\t\tb->booltesttype = IS_TRUE;\n\t\t\t\t\tb->location = @2;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) b;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| a_expr IS NOT TRUE_P\t\t\t\t\t\t%prec IS\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tBooleanTest *b = makeNode(BooleanTest);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tb->arg = (Expr *) $1;\n\t\t\t\t\tb->booltesttype = IS_NOT_TRUE;\n\t\t\t\t\tb->location = @2;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) b;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| a_expr IS FALSE_P\t\t\t\t\t\t\t%prec IS\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tBooleanTest *b = makeNode(BooleanTest);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tb->arg = (Expr *) UNKNOWN\t\t\t\t\t\t\t%prec IS\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tBooleanTest *b = makeNode(BooleanTest);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tb->arg = (Expr *) $1;\n\t\t\t\t\tb->booltesttype = IS_UNKNOWN;\n\t\t\t\t\tb->location = @2;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) b;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| a_expr IS NOT UNKNOWN\t\t\t\t\t\t%prec IS\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tBooleanTest *b = makeNode(BooleanTest);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tb->arg = (Expr *) $1;\n\t\t\t\t\tb->booltesttype = IS_NOT_UNKNOWN;\n\t\t\t\t\tb->location = @2;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) b;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| a_expr IS DISTINCT FROM a_expr\t\t\t%prec IS\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_DISTINCT, \"=\", $1, $5, @2);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| a_expr IS NOT DISTINCT FROM a_expr\t\t%prec IS\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_NOT_DISTINCT, \"=\", $1, $6, @2);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| a_expr BETWEEN opt_asymmetric b_expr AND a_expr\t\t%prec BETWEEN\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_BETWEEN,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   \"BETWEEN\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   $1,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   (Node *) list_make2($4, $6),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   @2);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| a_expr NOT_LA BETWEEN opt_asymmetric b_expr AND a_expr %prec NOT_LA\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_NOT_BETWEEN,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   \"NOT BETWEEN\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   $1,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   (Node *) list_make2($5, $7),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   @2);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| a_expr BETWEEN SYMMETRIC b_expr AND a_expr\t\t\t%prec BETWEEN\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_BETWEEN_SYM,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   \"BETWEEN SYMMETRIC\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   $1,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   (Node *) list_make2($4, $6),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   @2);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| a_expr NOT_LA BETWEEN SYMMETRIC b_expr AND a_expr\t\t%prec NOT_LA\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_NOT_BETWEEN_SYM,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   \"NOT BETWEEN SYMMETRIC\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   $1,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   (Node *) list_make2($5, $7),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   @2);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| a_expr IN_P select_with_parens\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t/* generate foo = ANY (subquery) */\n\t\t\t\t\tSubLink\t   *n = makeNode(SubLink);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->subselect = $3;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->subLinkType = ANY_SUBLINK;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->subLinkId = 0;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->testexpr = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->operName = NIL;\t\t/* show it's IN not = ANY */\n\t\t\t\t\tn->location = @2;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| a_expr IN_P '(' expr_list ')'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t/* generate scalar IN expression */\n\t\t\t\t\tA_Expr *n = makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_IN, \"=\", $1, (Node *) $4, @2);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->rexpr_list_start = @3;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->rexpr_list_end = @5;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| a_expr NOT_LA IN_P select_with_parens\t\t\t%prec NOT_LA\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t/* generate NOT (foo = ANY (subquery)) */\n\t\t\t\t\tSubLink\t   *n = makeNode(SubLink);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->subselect = $4;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->subLinkType = ANY_SUBLINK;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->subLinkId = 0;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->testexpr = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->operName = NIL;\t\t/* show it's IN not = ANY */\n\t\t\t\t\tn->location = @2;\n\t\t\t\t\t/* Stick a NOT on top; must have same parse location */\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeNotExpr((Node *) n, @2);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| a_expr NOT_LA IN_P '(' expr_list ')'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t/* generate scalar NOT IN expression */\n\t\t\t\t\tA_Expr *n = makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_IN, \"<>\", $1, (Node *) $5, @2);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->rexpr_list_start = @4;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->rexpr_list_end = @6;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| a_expr subquery_Op sub_type select_with_parens\t%prec Op\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tSubLink\t   *n = makeNode(SubLink);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->subLinkType = $3;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->subLinkId = 0;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->testexpr = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->operName = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->subselect = $4;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->location = @2;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| a_expr subquery_Op sub_type '(' a_expr ')'\t\t%prec Op\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tif ($3 == ANY_SUBLINK)\n\t\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) makeA_Expr(AEXPR_OP_ANY, $2, $1, $5, @2);\n\t\t\t\t\telse\n\t\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) makeA_Expr(AEXPR_OP_ALL, $2, $1, $5, @2);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| UNIQUE opt_unique_null_treatment select_with_parens\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t/* Not sure how to get rid of the parentheses\n\t\t\t\t\t * but there are lots of shift/reduce errors without them.\n\t\t\t\t\t *\n\t\t\t\t\t * Should be able to implement this by plopping the entire\n\t\t\t\t\t * select into a node, then transforming the target expressions\n\t\t\t\t\t * from whatever they are into count(*), and testing the\n\t\t\t\t\t * entire result equal to one.\n\t\t\t\t\t * But, will probably implement a separate node in the executor.\n\t\t\t\t\t */\n\t\t\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"UNIQUE predicate is not yet implemented\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t parser_errposition(@1)));\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| a_expr IS DOCUMENT_P\t\t\t\t\t%prec IS\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeXmlExpr(IS_DOCUMENT, NULL, NIL,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t list_make1($1), @2);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| a_expr IS NOT DOCUMENT_P\t\t\t\t%prec IS\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeNotExpr(makeXmlExpr(IS_DOCUMENT, NULL, NIL,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t list_make1($1), @2),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t @2);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| a_expr IS NORMALIZED\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t%prec IS\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) makeFuncCall(SystemFuncName(\"is_normalized\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   list_make1($1),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   COERCE_SQL_SYNTAX,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   @2);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| a_expr IS unicode_normal_form NORMALIZED\t\t\t%prec IS\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) makeFuncCall(SystemFuncName(\"is_normalized\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   list_make2($1, makeStringConst($3, @3)),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   COERCE_SQL_SYNTAX,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   @2);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| a_expr IS NOT NORMALIZED\t\t\t\t\t\t\t%prec IS\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeNotExpr((Node *) makeFuncCall(SystemFuncName(\"is_normalized\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   list_make1($1),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   COERCE_SQL_SYNTAX,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   @2),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t @2);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| a_expr IS NOT unicode_normal_form NORMALIZED\t\t%prec IS\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeNotExpr((Node *) makeFuncCall(SystemFuncName(\"is_normalized\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   list_make2($1, makeStringConst($4, @4)),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   COERCE_SQL_SYNTAX,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   @2),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t @2);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| a_expr IS json_predicate_type_constraint\n\t\t\t\t\tjson_key_uniqueness_constraint_opt\t\t%prec IS\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tJsonFormat *format = makeJsonFormat(JS_FORMAT_DEFAULT, JS_ENC_DEFAULT, -1);\n\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeJsonIsPredicate($1, format, $3, $4, @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t/*\n\t\t\t * Required by SQL/JSON, but there are conflicts\n\t\t\t| a_expr\n\t\t\t\tjson_format_clause\n\t\t\t\tIS  json_predicate_type_constraint\n\t\t\t\t\tjson_key_uniqueness_constraint_opt\t\t%prec IS\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeJsonIsPredicate($1, $2, $4, $5, @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t*/\n\t\t\t| a_expr IS NOT\n\t\t\t\t\tjson_predicate_type_constraint\n\t\t\t\t\tjson_key_uniqueness_constraint_opt\t\t%prec IS\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tJsonFormat *format = makeJsonFormat(JS_FORMAT_DEFAULT, JS_ENC_DEFAULT, -1);\n\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeNotExpr(makeJsonIsPredicate($1, format, $4, $5, @1), @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t/*\n\t\t\t * Required by SQL/JSON, but there are conflicts\n\t\t\t| a_expr\n\t\t\t\tjson_format_clause\n\t\t\t\tIS NOT\n\t\t\t\t\tjson_predicate_type_constraint\n\t\t\t\t\tjson_key_uniqueness_constraint_opt\t\t%prec IS\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeNotExpr(makeJsonIsPredicate($1, $2, $5, $6, @1), @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t*/\n\t\t\t| DEFAULT\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t/*\n\t\t\t\t\t * The SQL spec only allows DEFAULT in \"contextually typed\n\t\t\t\t\t * expressions\", but for us, it's easier to allow it in\n\t\t\t\t\t * any a_expr and then throw error during parse analysis\n\t\t\t\t\t * if it's in an inappropriate context.  This way also\n\t\t\t\t\t * lets us say something smarter than \"syntax error\".\n\t\t\t\t\t */\n\t\t\t\t\tSetToDefault *n = makeNode(SetToDefault);\n\n\t\t\t\t\t/* parse analysis will fill in the rest */\n\t\t\t\t\tn->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\n/*\n * Restricted expressions\n *\n * b_expr is a subset of the complete expression syntax defined by a_expr.\n *\n * Presently, AND, NOT, IS, and IN are the a_expr keywords that would\n * cause trouble in the places where b_expr is used.  For simplicity, we\n * just eliminate all the boolean-keyword-operator productions from b_expr.\n */\nb_expr:\t\tc_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| b_expr TYPECAST Typename\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = makeTypeCast($1, $3, @2); }\n\t\t\t| '+' b_expr\t\t\t\t\t%prec UMINUS\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, \"+\", NULL, $2, @1); }\n\t\t\t| '-' b_expr\t\t\t\t\t%prec UMINUS\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = doNegate($2, @1); }\n\t\t\t| b_expr '+' b_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, \"+\", $1, $3, @2); }\n\t\t\t| b_expr '-' b_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, \"-\", $1, $3, @2); }\n\t\t\t| b_expr '*' b_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, \"*\", $1, $3, @2); }\n\t\t\t| b_expr '/' b_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, \"/\", $1, $3, @2); }\n\t\t\t| b_expr '%' b_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, \"%\", $1, $3, @2); }\n\t\t\t| b_expr '^' b_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, \"^\", $1, $3, @2); }\n\t\t\t| b_expr '<' b_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, \"<\", $1, $3, @2); }\n\t\t\t| b_expr '>' b_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, \">\", $1, $3, @2); }\n\t\t\t| b_expr '=' b_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, \"=\", $1, $3, @2); }\n\t\t\t| b_expr LESS_EQUALS b_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, \"<=\", $1, $3, @2); }\n\t\t\t| b_expr GREATER_EQUALS b_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, \">=\", $1, $3, @2); }\n\t\t\t| b_expr NOT_EQUALS b_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, \"<>\", $1, $3, @2); }\n\t\t\t| b_expr qual_Op b_expr\t\t\t\t%prec Op\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = (Node *) makeA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, $2, $1, $3, @2); }\n\t\t\t| qual_Op b_expr\t\t\t\t\t%prec Op\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = (Node *) makeA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, $1, NULL, $2, @1); }\n\t\t\t| b_expr IS DISTINCT FROM b_expr\t\t%prec IS\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_DISTINCT, \"=\", $1, $5, @2);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| b_expr IS NOT DISTINCT FROM b_expr\t%prec IS\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_NOT_DISTINCT, \"=\", $1, $6, @2);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| b_expr IS DOCUMENT_P\t\t\t\t\t%prec IS\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeXmlExpr(IS_DOCUMENT, NULL, NIL,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t list_make1($1), @2);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| b_expr IS NOT DOCUMENT_P\t\t\t\t%prec IS\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeNotExpr(makeXmlExpr(IS_DOCUMENT, NULL, NIL,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t list_make1($1), @2),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t @2);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\n/*\n * Productions that can be used in both a_expr and b_expr.\n *\n * Note: productions that refer recursively to a_expr or b_expr mostly\n * cannot appear here.\tHowever, it's OK to refer to a_exprs that occur\n * inside parentheses, such as function arguments; that cannot introduce\n * ambiguity to the b_expr syntax.\n */\nc_expr:\t\tcolumnref\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| AexprConst\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| PARAM opt_indirection\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tParamRef   *p = makeNode(ParamRef);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tp->number = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\tp->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t\tif ($2)\n\t\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t\tA_Indirection *n = makeNode(A_Indirection);\n\n\t\t\t\t\t\tn->arg = (Node *) p;\n\t\t\t\t\t\tn->indirection = check_indirection($2, yyscanner);\n\t\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\telse\n\t\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) p;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| '(' a_expr ')' opt_indirection\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tif ($4)\n\t\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t\tA_Indirection *n = makeNode(A_Indirection);\n\n\t\t\t\t\t\tn->arg = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\t\tn->indirection = check_indirection($4, yyscanner);\n\t\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\telse\n\t\t\t\t\t\t$$ = $2;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| case_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| func_expr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| select_with_parens\t\t\t%prec UMINUS\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tSubLink\t   *n = makeNode(SubLink);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->subLinkType = EXPR_SUBLINK;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->subLinkId = 0;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->testexpr = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->operName = NIL;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->subselect = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| select_with_parens indirection\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t/*\n\t\t\t\t\t * Because the select_with_parens nonterminal is designed\n\t\t\t\t\t * to \"eat\" as many levels of parens as possible, the\n\t\t\t\t\t * '(' a_expr ')' opt_indirection production above will\n\t\t\t\t\t * fail to match a sub-SELECT with indirection decoration;\n\t\t\t\t\t * the sub-SELECT won't be regarded as an a_expr as long\n\t\t\t\t\t * as there are parens around it.  To support applying\n\t\t\t\t\t * subscripting or field selection to a sub-SELECT result,\n\t\t\t\t\t * we need this redundant-looking production.\n\t\t\t\t\t */\n\t\t\t\t\tSubLink\t   *n = makeNode(SubLink);\n\t\t\t\t\tA_Indirection *a = makeNode(A_Indirection);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->subLinkType = EXPR_SUBLINK;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->subLinkId = 0;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->testexpr = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->operName = NIL;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->subselect = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t\ta->arg = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t\ta->indirection = check_indirection($2, yyscanner);\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) a;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| EXISTS select_with_parens\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tSubLink\t   *n = makeNode(SubLink);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->subLinkType = EXISTS_SUBLINK;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->subLinkId = 0;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->testexpr = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->operName = NIL;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->subselect = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| ARRAY select_with_parens\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tSubLink\t   *n = makeNode(SubLink);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->subLinkType = ARRAY_SUBLINK;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->subLinkId = 0;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->testexpr = NULL;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->operName = NIL;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->subselect = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| ARRAY array_expr\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tA_ArrayExpr *n = castNode(A_ArrayExpr, $2);\n\n\t\t\t\t\t/* point outermost A_ArrayExpr to the ARRAY keyword */\n\t\t\t\t\tn->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| explicit_row\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tRowExpr\t   *r = makeNode(RowExpr);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tr->args = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\tr->row_typeid = InvalidOid;\t/* not analyzed yet */\n\t\t\t\t\tr->colnames = NIL;\t/* to be filled in during analysis */\n\t\t\t\t\tr->row_format = COERCE_EXPLICIT_CALL; /* abuse */\n\t\t\t\t\tr->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) r;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| implicit_row\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tRowExpr\t   *r = makeNode(RowExpr);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tr->args = $1;\n\t\t\t\t\tr->row_typeid = InvalidOid;\t/* not analyzed yet */\n\t\t\t\t\tr->colnames = NIL;\t/* to be filled in during analysis */\n\t\t\t\t\tr->row_format = COERCE_IMPLICIT_CAST; /* abuse */\n\t\t\t\t\tr->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) r;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| GROUPING '(' expr_list ')'\n\t\t\t  {\n\t\t\t\t  GroupingFunc *g = makeNode(GroupingFunc);\n\n\t\t\t\t  g->args = $3;\n\t\t\t\t  g->location = @1;\n\t\t\t\t  $$ = (Node *) g;\n\t\t\t  }\n\t\t;\n\nfunc_application: func_name '(' ')'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) makeFuncCall($1, NIL,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   COERCE_EXPLICIT_CALL,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| func_name '(' func_arg_list opt_sort_clause ')'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tFuncCall   *n = makeFuncCall($1, $3,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t COERCE_EXPLICIT_CALL,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t @1);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->agg_order = $4;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| func_name '(' VARIADIC func_arg_expr opt_sort_clause ')'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tFuncCall   *n = makeFuncCall($1, list_make1($4),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t COERCE_EXPLICIT_CALL,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t @1);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->func_variadic = true;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->agg_order = $5;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| func_name '(' func_arg_list ',' VARIADIC func_arg_expr opt_sort_clause ')'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tFuncCall   *n = makeFuncCall($1, lappend($3, $6),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t COERCE_EXPLICIT_CALL,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t @1);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->func_variadic = true;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->agg_order = $7;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| func_name '(' ALL func_arg_list opt_sort_clause ')'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tFuncCall   *n = makeFuncCall($1, $4,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t COERCE_EXPLICIT_CALL,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t @1);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->agg_order = $5;\n\t\t\t\t\t/* Ideally we'd mark the FuncCall node to indicate\n\t\t\t\t\t * \"must be an aggregate\", but there's no provision\n\t\t\t\t\t * for that in FuncCall at the moment.\n\t\t\t\t\t */\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| func_name '(' DISTINCT func_arg_list opt_sort_clause ')'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tFuncCall   *n = makeFuncCall($1, $4,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t COERCE_EXPLICIT_CALL,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t @1);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->agg_order = $5;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->agg_distinct = true;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| func_name '(' '*' ')'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t/*\n\t\t\t\t\t * We consider AGGREGATE(*) to invoke a parameterless\n\t\t\t\t\t * aggregate.  This does the right thing for COUNT(*),\n\t\t\t\t\t * and there are no other aggregates in SQL that accept\n\t\t\t\t\t * '*' as parameter.\n\t\t\t\t\t *\n\t\t\t\t\t * The FuncCall node is also marked agg_star = true,\n\t\t\t\t\t * so that later processing can detect what the argument\n\t\t\t\t\t * really was.\n\t\t\t\t\t */\n\t\t\t\t\tFuncCall   *n = makeFuncCall($1, NIL,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t COERCE_EXPLICIT_CALL,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t @1);\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->agg_star = true;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t;\n\n\n/*\n * func_expr and its cousin func_expr_windowless are split out from c_expr just\n * so that we have classifications for \"everything that is a function call or\n * looks like one\".  This isn't very important, but it saves us having to\n * document which variants are legal in places like \"FROM function()\" or the\n * backwards-compatible functional-index syntax for CREATE INDEX.\n * (Note that many of the special SQL functions wouldn't actually make any\n * sense as functional index entries, but we ignore that consideration here.)\n */\nfunc_expr: func_application within_group_clause filter_clause over_clause\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tFuncCall   *n = (FuncCall *) $1;\n\n\t\t\t\t\t/*\n\t\t\t\t\t * The order clause for WITHIN GROUP and the one for\n\t\t\t\t\t * plain-aggregate ORDER BY share a field, so we have to\n\t\t\t\t\t * check here that at most one is present.  We also check\n\t\t\t\t\t * for DISTINCT and VARIADIC here to give a better error\n\t\t\t\t\t * location.  Other consistency checks are deferred to\n\t\t\t\t\t * parse analysis.\n\t\t\t\t\t */\n\t\t\t\t\tif ($2 != NIL)\n\t\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t\tif (n->agg_order != NIL)\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"cannot use multiple ORDER BY clauses with WITHIN GROUP\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t parser_errposition(@2)));\n\t\t\t\t\t\tif (n->agg_distinct)\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"cannot use DISTINCT with WITHIN GROUP\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t parser_errposition(@2)));\n\t\t\t\t\t\tif (n->func_variadic)\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tereport(ERROR,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t errmsg(\"cannot use VARIADIC with WITHIN GROUP\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t parser_errposition(@2)));\n\t\t\t\t\t\tn->agg_order = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\t\tn->agg_within_group = true;\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\tn->agg_filter = $3;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->over = $4;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) n;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| json_aggregate_func filter_clause over_clause\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tJsonAggConstructor *n = IsA($1, JsonObjectAgg) ?\n\t\t\t\t\t\t((JsonObjectAgg *) $1)->constructor :\n\t\t\t\t\t\t((JsonArrayAgg *) $1)->constructor;\n\n\t\t\t\t\tn->agg_filter = $2;\n\t\t\t\t\tn->over = $3;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) $1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| func_expr_common_subexpr\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t;\n\n/*\n * Like func_expr but does not accept WINDOW functions directly\n * (but they can still be contained in arguments for functions etc).\n * Use this when window expressions are not allowed, where needed to\n * disambiguate the grammar (e.g. in CREATE INDEX).\n */\nfunc_expr_windowless:\n\t\t\tfunc_application\t\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| func_expr_common_subexpr\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t\t| json_aggregate_func\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = $1; }\n\t\t;\n\n/*\n * Special expressions that are considered to be functions.\n */\nfunc_expr_common_subexpr:\n\t\t\tCOLLATION FOR '(' a_expr ')'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) makeFuncCall(SystemFuncName(\"pg_collation_for\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   list_make1($4),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   COERCE_SQL_SYNTAX,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| CURRENT_DATE\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeSQLValueFunction(SVFOP_CURRENT_DATE, -1, @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| -1, @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| CAST '(' a_expr AS Typename ')'\n\t\t\t\t{ $$ = makeTypeCast($3, $5, @1); }\n\t\t\t| EXTRACT '(' extract_list ')'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) ')'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) makeFuncCall(SystemFuncName(\"normalize\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   list_make1($3),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   COERCE_SQL_SYNTAX,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t makeStringConst($5, @5)),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   COERCE_SQL_SYNTAX,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| OVERLAY '(' overlay_list ')'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) makeFuncCall(SystemFuncName(\"overlay\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   $3,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   COERCE_SQL_SYNTAX,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| OVERLAY '(' func_arg_list_opt ')'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t/*\n\t\t\t\t\t * allow functions named overlay() to be called without\n\t\t\t\t\t * special syntax\n\t\t\t\t\t */\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) makeFuncCall(list_make1(makeString(\"overlay\")),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   $3,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   COERCE_EXPLICIT_CALL,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| POSITION '(' position_list ')'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t/*\n\t\t\t\t\t * position(A in B) is converted to position(B, A)\n\t\t\t\t\t *\n\t\t\t\t\t * We deliberately don't offer a \"plain syntax\" option\n\t\t\t\t\t * for position(), because the reversal of the arguments\n\t\t\t\t\t * creates too much risk of confusion.\n\t\t\t\t\t */\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) makeFuncCall(SystemFuncName(\"position\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   $3,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   COERCE_SQL_SYNTAX,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| SUBSTRING '(' substr_list ')'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t/* substring(A from B for C) is converted to\n\t\t\t\t\t * substring(A, B, C) - thomas 2000-11-28\n\t\t\t\t\t */\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) makeFuncCall(SystemFuncName(\"substring\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   $3,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   COERCE_SQL_SYNTAX,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| SUBSTRING '(' func_arg_list_opt ')'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t/*\n\t\t\t\t\t * allow functions named substring() to be called without\n\t\t\t\t\t * special syntax\n\t\t\t\t\t */\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) makeFuncCall(list_make1(makeString(\"substring\")),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   $3,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   COERCE_EXPLICIT_CALL,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| TREAT '(' a_expr AS Typename ')'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t/* TREAT(expr AS target) converts expr of a particular type to target,\n\t\t\t\t\t * which is defined to be a subtype of the original expression.\n\t\t\t\t\t * In SQL99, this is intended for use with structured UDTs,\n\t\t\t\t\t * but let's make this a generally useful form allowing stronger\n\t\t\t\t\t * coercions than are handled by implicit casting.\n\t\t\t\t\t *\n\t\t\t\t\t * Convert SystemTypeName() to SystemFuncName() even though\n\t\t\t\t\t * at the moment they result in the same thing.\n\t\t\t\t\t */\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) makeFuncCall(SystemFuncName(strVal(llast($5->names))),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   list_make1($3),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   COERCE_EXPLICIT_CALL,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| TRIM '(' BOTH trim_list ')'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t/* various trim expressions are defined in SQL\n\t\t\t\t\t * - thomas 1997-07-19\n\t\t\t\t\t */\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) makeFuncCall(SystemFuncName(\"btrim\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   $4,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   COERCE_SQL_SYNTAX,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   @1);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t| TRIM '(' LEADING trim_list ')'\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = (Node *) makeFuncCall(SystemFuncName(\"ltrim\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   $4,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t   COERCE_SQL_SYNTAX,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t $3; }\n\t\t;\n\nxml_attribute_list:\txml_attribute_el\t\t\t\t\t{ $$ = list_make1($1); }\n\t\t\t| xml_attribute_list ',' xml_attribute_el\t{ $$ = lappend($1, $3); }\n\t\t;\n\nxml_attribute_el: a_expr AS ColLabel\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t$$ = makeNode(ResTarget);\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->name = $3;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->indirection = NIL;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->val = (Node *) $1;\n\t\t\t\t\t$$->location =
